info.title: Businessmap API v2
info.description:
[Contact Support](https://businessmap.io/customer-support) [Apache 2.0](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0) ## Table of Contents - 🔑 **[Authentication](#/#authentication)** - ⚙️ **[API Request Limits](#/#api-request-limits)** - ⏳ [Types of Limits](#/#types-of-limits) - ⚡ [Default Limits](#/#default-limits) - 📝 [Response Headers Related to Limits](#/#response-headers-related-to-limits) - 🚫 [Exceeding the Limits](#/#exceeding-the-limits) - 🚨 **[Errors](#/#errors)** - 🏷️ **[Response Headers](#/#response-headers)** - 🔍 **[Filtering and Expanding Resources](#/#filtering-and-expanding-resources)** - 🔎 [Filtering](#/#filtering) - ↗️ [Expanding](#/#expanding) - 📦 **[Pagination](#/#pagination)** - 🔢 [Pagination Query Parameters](#/#pagination-query-parameters) - 🚀 **[Optimisation](#/#optimisation)** ## Authentication Authentication is performed using an alphanumeric API key that must be included in the request header. You can find your existing API key or generate a new one by going to your **Businessmap account** and in the top-right corner clicking **My Account** -> **API**:
Once you have opened the API section, you can click **Show API key** to view it, or **Copy to clipboard** to easily copy the key for use in your requests. > ### Important > > Please note that if you generate a new API key, your current one will be **permanently** deleted, which may affect any existing integrations that rely on it. > This operation is **permanent** and **cannot** be undone. ## API Request Limits These API limits determine how many API calls you can make within a given time period. There are two types of request limits: **hourly** and **minutely**. For more information on the API limits for your account, you can call the **[API Limits](#/operations/getApiLimits#Request)** endpoint. ### Types of Limits - **Hourly** Limit - The number of requests allowed within one hour. - **Minutely** Limit - The number of requests allowed within one minute. ### Default Limits - The default **hourly** limit is set to `600` requests. - The default **minutely** limit is set to `30` requests. ### Response Headers Related to Limits The following **response headers** also include information about the API limits: - `X-RateLimit-PerHour-Limit` - Indicates the maximum number of requests allowed per hour. - `X-RateLimit-PerHour-Remaining` - Shows the number of requests remaining in the current hour. - `X-RateLimit-PerMinute-Limit` - Denotes the maximum number of requests allowed per minute. - `X-RateLimit-PerMinute-Remaining` - Indicates the number of requests remaining in the current minute. ### Exceeding the Limits If you exceed these limits, you may receive an error response indicating that the rate limit has been reached. ```json { "error": { "code": "RL02", "message": "You have exceeded the minutely request limit. The limit is 10 and you've made 12 requests prior to this one. You can try again after 58 seconds.", "reference": "GE-RL02", "details": { "limit": 10, "requests": 12, "retry_after": 58 } } } ``` #### Parameter breakdown: - `limit` - Denotes the allowed number of requests for the selected endpoint within the specified time window. In this case, the limit is **10 requests** per minute. - `requests` - Indicates how many requests have been made so far. For this example, **12 requests** have been made prior to the current request attempt. - `retry_after` - Specifies the number of seconds remaining until you can make another successful request. In this example, you must wait **58 seconds** before trying again. ## Errors When interacting with the API, various errors may occur. These errors will be returned as part of the response body in an error object. The possible errors that can be returned are shown in the table below: Code | Type | Description ---------|----------|--------- 400 | Bad Request | The request failed due to a client error. 401 | Unauthorized | You are not authorized to access the API. 403 | Forbidden | You do not have the permissions required to access this resource or to perform this action. 404 | Not Found | This resource does not exist at the moment. 409 | Conflict | The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the resource. 429 | Too Many Requests | This request will not be processed because you have sent too many requests recently. 500 | Internal Server Error | The request failed due to an internal server error. 503 | Service Unavailable | The service is temporarily unavailable. ```json { "error": { "code": "B001", "message": "A board with id 1'204'9702 does not exist.", "reference": "GE-B001", "details": { "board_id": 1'204'9702 } } } ``` #### Parameter breakdown: - `code` - A unique 4-character code associated with the error. - `message` - A description of the error. - `reference` - A reference that you can use if you need to [contact customer support](https://businessmap.io/customer-support) about the error. - `details` - Details specific to the error. Such as the values that caused it. > ### Info > > Please note that sometimes the API response may lack the `details` object in generic error scenarios, e.g. accessing a board with an invalid board ID. ## Response Headers The following **response headers** are included in the API response for **every** successful request: - `X-RateLimit-PerHour-Limit` - Indicates the maximum number of requests allowed per hour. - `X-RateLimit-PerHour-Remaining` - Shows the number of requests remaining in the current hour. - `X-RateLimit-PerMinute-Limit` - Denotes the maximum number of requests allowed per minute. - `X-RateLimit-PerMinute-Remaining` - Indicates the number of requests remaining in the current minute. - `X-Kanbanize-Version` - Shows the current version of Businessmap. ## Filtering and Expanding Resources By using both expand and filters, you can refine your queries and retrieve more detailed and relevant data. Filters allow you to narrow down results based on specific criteria, ensuring that only the most relevant information is returned. Expand is used to get more details about a specific object that is returned for an endpoint without having to call a separate endpoint. > ### Info > > Please note that filtering and expanding are **not** available for all endpoints. If they are available, they will be specified as **query parameters** for the selected endpoint. > >Invalid or unsupported filters provided in `GET` requests are **ignored**. The request will still be processed and no error will be returned. ### Filtering Filters are applied to refine the data returned by the API, allowing you to focus on specific attributes or values that match your requirements. Common filters include: - `from` - Filters results that occurred after a specific date and time in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format. - `to` - Filters results that occurred before a specific date and time in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format. - `from_date` - Filters results that occurred after a specific date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. - `to_date` - Filters results that occurred before a specific date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. ### Expanding When expanding is enabled, you can request additional details about specific properties in the response. To do so, simply list the properties available for the selected endpoint you wish to expand as query parameters, e.g. `expand=custom_fields`. ```json { "data": { "pagination": { "all_pages": 1, "current_page": 1, "results_per_page": 200 }, "data": [ { "card_id": 539522, "custom_id": null, "board_id": 1075, "workflow_id": 1695, "title": "childInitiative1", "owner_user_id": null, "type_id": null, "color": "34a97b", "section": 2, "column_id": 15876, "lane_id": 9838, "position": 2, "custom_fields": [ { "field_id": 638, "value": 50, "display_value": "50" } ] } ] } } ``` ## Pagination Pagination is used to split large sets of results into smaller, more manageable pages. It allows users to retrieve a subset of results at a time, improving performance and providing a more user-friendly experience when dealing with extensive data sets. If pagination is available results are **always** returned in pages. > ### Info > > Please note that pagination is **not** available for all endpoints. If it is available, the specific endpoint will list the available query parameters. > Additionally in the response body, a distinct `pagination` object will appear at the top, containing the relevant pagination details. ### Pagination Query Parameters You can control the pagination behavior using the following query parameters: - `page` - This parameter specifies which page of results to return. By default, the API returns the **first** page. - `per_page` - This controls the number of results per page. The **default** value is `100`, and the **maximum** allowed is `200`. By using these parameters, you can navigate through large datasets more effectively. ```json { "pagination": { "all_pages": 199387, "current_page": 1, "results_per_page": 1 }, "data": [ { "user_id": 2, "resource": "api/v*/cards/*", "method": "get", "status_code": 404, "support_reference": null, "time": "2025-01-01T00:00:00+00:00" } ] } ``` ## Optimisation When utilising the API, it's important to design your requests efficiently to reduce latency, lower network load, and improve overall performance. Many resources in the Businessmap API include a main GET endpoint (e.g. [GET /cards](#/operations/getCards), [GET /boards](#/operations/getBoards), etc.) that supports filtering, expanding, and field selection parameters. These features are specifically designed to help you retrieve all the information you need in a single, optimised request, instead of sending multiple individual calls such as [GET /cards/{card_id}](#/operations/getCard ) for each item. Employing the collection endpoints with supported query parameters (e.g. `expand`, `fields` and various other filters **specific** to each endpoint) enables more efficient data retrieval, reduces request overhead, and enhances API performance. For example, instead of making several separate calls to fetch detailed data for each card: ``` GET /cards/101 GET /cards/102 GET /cards/103 ``` You can use the main endpoint once and expand the required properties: ``` GET /cards?card_ids=101,102,103 ``` This not only returns all relevant data in one response but also ensures your integration runs faster and scales better. In addition, when creating or updating multiple resources, you can use the corresponding bulk endpoints, such as [POST /cards/createMany](#/operations/createManyCards), to perform batch operations in a single request. Using these bulk endpoints helps optimise both performance and reliability across your integration.

get.summary: Get the hourly and minutely limits for API requests
get.description:
Retrieves a list of API limits that determine how many API calls you can make within a given time period. There are two types of request limits: **hourly** and **minutely**.      

### Types of Limits

- **Hourly** Limit - The number of requests allowed within one hour.
- **Minutely** Limit - The number of requests allowed within one minute.

### Default Limits

- The default **hourly** limit is set to `600` requests.
- The default **minutely** limit is set to `30` requests.

### Response Headers Related to Limits

The following **response headers** also include information about the API limits:

- `X-RateLimit-PerHour-Limit` - Indicates the maximum number of requests allowed per hour.

- `X-RateLimit-PerHour-Remaining` - Shows the number of requests remaining in the current hour.

- `X-RateLimit-PerMinute-Limit` - Denotes the maximum number of requests allowed per minute.

- `X-RateLimit-PerMinute-Remaining` - Indicates the number of requests remaining in the current minute.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get a list of past API requests
get.description:
Retrieves a list of previously made API requests along with their relevant details.
This endpoint allows you to review the history of API usage and can be tailored using various filtering options.

parameters.name: user_ids
parameters.description: A list of user IDs used to filter the API request history to include only requests made by those users.

parameters.name: methods
parameters.description: A list of HTTP methods used to filter the API request history to include only requests executed with those methods.

parameters.name: status_codes
parameters.description: A list of HTTP status codes used to filter the API request history to include only responses with those status codes.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The first date and time from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The last date and time from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: The first date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: The last date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Results are **always** paginated and returned in pages. This parameter controls which page is returned. Defaults to the **first** page if not specified.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Controls how many results are returned per page. The default value is `100` and the maximum is `200`.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of past API requests.

This endpoint allows you to review the history of API usage and can be tailored using various filtering options.

parameters.description: The first date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: The last date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: An aggregated response of the API's history, grouped by User ID.

get.summary: Get a list of archived card versions
get.description:
Get a list of all created archived card versions. Archived card versions provide a way to group multiple archived cards together under a single version, which can either be **manually** or **automatically** created.

 - **Manually** - Archived card versions can be manually created via **[POST request](#/operations/createArchiveCardVersion#Request)**. 

 - **Automatically** - Archived card versions are automatically generated when cards are archived based on board settings. Cards may be automatically archived every `1`, `7`, `14`, or `30` days and a **single** archived card version is created for each automatic archiving operation.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of archived card versions.

post.description:
Create a new archived card version. The version name must be **unique**, as duplicate names are **not** permitted.
To associate a card with an archived card version, the `version_id` **must** be specified when the card is being archived via **[PATCH request](#/operations/updateCard#Request)**.

If a card is **already** archived, it has to be **unarchived** before it can be associated with a new archived card version.

schema.description: Archived card version data.

name.description: The name of the new version.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get the details of a single archived card version
get.description: Get the details of a single archived card version.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

patch.summary: Update an archived card version
patch.description: Update an archived card version. Only the **name** of the archived card version can be updated.

schema.description: Archived card version data.

name.description: The name of the version.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Delete an archived card version
delete.description:
Delete an archived card version.

<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>
> This does **not** delete the archived card itself, rather the version under which the archived card is grouped.

204.description: The archived card version has been deleted.

get.summary: Get a list of BAI prompt creation, deletion, and update events.
get.description:
Returns a log of all actions related to the prompts within Businessmap AI (BAI). This includes records of prompts that have been **created**, **updated**, or **deleted**.

There are **three** types of prompts:

- **Comments and Emails** - Prompts that allow the generation of text content for a comment or email 
- **Subtasks** -  Prompts that generate text for comments or email content associated with an existing card.
- **Summaries** - Prompts that generate a concise summary of a card's contents, such as its title, description, and other relevant details.

parameters.name: prompt_ids
parameters.description: A list of BAI prompt IDs by which to filter the BAI prompt history.

parameters.name: user_ids
parameters.description: A list of user IDs used to filter the BAI prompts history to include only requests made by those users.

parameters.name: event_types
parameters.description: A list of event types by which to filter the BAI prompt history.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The first date and time from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The last date and time from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: The first date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: The last date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Results are **always** paginated and returned in pages. This parameter controls which page is returned. Defaults to the **first** page if not specified.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Controls how many results are returned per page. The default value is `100` and the maximum is `200`.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get a list of boards where the block reason is available
get.description:
Get a list of board IDs where the specified block reason is applicable. A list of all block reasons can be retrieved by calling the [blockReasons endpoint](#/operations/getBlockReasons#Request).

The **Waiting on us** and **Waiting on others** block reasons exist by default for **every** account. As such, they are available for **all** boards (unless specified otherwise) along with the **Generic Blocker**.

<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>
> The **Generic Blocker** is **not** included in this list. To determine if a board allows its use, you can call the [currentStructure endpoint](#/paths/boards-board_id--currentStructure/get#Request), which returns the `allow_generic_blocker` parameter.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: Contains the IDs of boards where the block reason is applicable.

board_id.description: The ID of a board where the block reason is applicable.

post.summary: Change the availability of a block reason on one or more boards
post.description: Change the availability of a block reason on one or more boards.

schema.description: Block reason boards data.

board_ids_to_add.description: A list of board IDs where the block reason should be made available.

board_ids_to_remove.description: A list of board IDs where the block reason should be made unavailable.

204.description: The block reason's availability has been updated on the specified board(s).

get.summary: Get a list of cards where the block reason is currently applied
get.description:
Get a list of card IDs where the specified block reason is **currently** applied. A list of all block reasons can be retrieved by calling the [blockReasons endpoint](#/operations/getBlockReasons#Request).

If a card is unblocked and then blocked again with a **different** block reason, it will **no longer** be associated with the initial block reason.
<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>
> The **Generic Blocker** is not included in this list. To determine if a card is currently blocked using the **Generic Blocker**, you can call the [cards/{card_id} endpoint](#/operations/getCard#Request).
> 
>If a card uses the **Generic Blocker** the `block_reason` object in the response body will have its `reason_id` parameter set to `null`.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: Contains the IDs of cards where the block reason is currently applied.

card_id.description: The ID of a card where the block reason is currently applied.

Retrieves a list of events related to block reasons.
This endpoint allows you to review the history of block reasons and can be tailored using various filtering options.

parameters.name: user_ids
parameters.description: A list of user IDs used to filter the block reasons history to include only events performed by those users.

parameters.name: event_types
parameters.description: A list of event types used to filter the block reasons history to include only matching events.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The first date and time from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The last date and time from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: The first date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: The last date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Results are **always** paginated and returned in pages. This parameter controls which page is returned. Defaults to the **first** page if not specified.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Controls how many results are returned per page. The default value is `100` and the maximum is `200`.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of block reasons creation, deletion and update events.

get.summary: Get a list of block reasons
get.description:
Get a list of all created block reasons. The **Waiting on us** and **Waiting on others** block reasons exist by default for **every** account.

A **Generic Blocker** is also available by default, but it is **not** listed as a block reason in this endpoint. To determine if a board allows its use, you can call the [currentStructure endpoint](#/paths/boards-board_id--currentStructure/get#Request), which returns the `allow_generic_blocker` parameter.

parameters.name: reason_ids
parameters.description: A list of reason IDs used to filter the block reasons to include those with the specified IDs.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Filters block reasons by their exact label (i.e. the name of the block reason).

parameters.name: availability
parameters.description:
A list of the availability levels by which to filter the block reasons.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the block reason can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the block reason is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the block reason is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a block reason's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

parameters.name: is_enabled
parameters.description: Filters block reasons based on whether they are enabled.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: A list of fields to include in the response.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties for which additional details should be included in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of block reasons.

post.description: Create a new block reason. The block reason name must be **unique**, as duplicate names are **not** permitted.

schema.description: Block reason data.

icon_type.description: The type of the icon associated with the block reason. `0` represents a system icon, while `1` represents a user-defined icon.

icon_id.description: The unique identifier for the icon associated with the block reason. If set to `0`, the block reason will not have an icon.

label.description: The **unique** label (i.e. the name) assigned to the block reason.

color.description: The color of the block reason in hexadecimal format.

availability.description:
The availability level of the block reason.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the block reason can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the block reason is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the block reason is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a block reason's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the block reason is enabled.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get the details of a single block reason
get.description:
Get the details of a single block reason.
<!-- theme: info -->

> #### Info
>
> You can use the [GET /blockReasons](#/operations/getBlockReasons) endpoint with the `reason_ids` query parameter to retrieve multiple block reasons in a single request. You can further refine the response by adding query parameters such as `fields` to return only specific attributes, or `expand` to include additional details about related resources.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

patch.summary: Update a block reason
patch.description: Update a block reason.

schema.description: Block reason data.

icon_type.description: The type of the icon associated with the block reason. `0` represents a system icon, while `1` represents a user-defined icon.

icon_id.description: The unique identifier for the icon associated with the block reason. If set to `0`, the block reason will not have an icon.

label.description: The **unique** label (i.e. the name) assigned to the block reason.

color.description: The color of the block reason in hexadecimal format.

availability.description:
The availability level of the block reason.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the block reason can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the block reason is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the block reason is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a block reason's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the block reason is enabled.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Delete a block reason
delete.description:
Deletes a block reason. Unless the `replace_with_reason_id` parameter is provided, **all** cards that were previously blocked by the deleted block reason will be **reassigned** to the **Generic Blocker**.

Additionally, a `comment` parameter will be automatically added to the **Generic Blocker**, containing the name of the deleted block reason. To view this update, you can call the [cards/{card_id} endpoint](#/operations/getCard#Request) and inspect the `block_reason` object in the response body.

#### Example:

- If a block reason named `"This is a blocker"` is deleted, the **Generic Blocker** will be applied to the affected cards, and a `comment` parameter will be included with the value `"This is a blocker"`.

parameters.name: replace_with_reason_id
parameters.description: The ID of the block reason that will replace the deleted block reason for **all** affected cards.

204.description: The block reason has been deleted.

post.description: Schedule multiple block reasons for deletion in a single request. Block reasons are not deleted immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the deletion runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Block reason IDs data.

reason_ids.description: A list of block reason IDs to be deleted.

204.description: The block reasons have been scheduled for deletion. The deletion runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

post.description: Schedule multiple block reasons for disabling in a single request. Block reasons are not disabled immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the disabling runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Block reason IDs data.

reason_ids.description: A list of block reason IDs to be disabled.

204.description: The block reasons have been scheduled for disabling. The operation runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

post.description: Schedule multiple block reasons for enabling in a single request. Block reasons are not enabled immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the enabling runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Block reason IDs data.

reason_ids.description: A list of block reason IDs to be enabled.

204.description: The block reasons have been scheduled for enabling. The operation runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

get.summary: Get a list of access requests for a board
get.description:
Get a list of user-submitted access requests for a specific board.

If a user has sent **multiple** access requests, only the **last** one will be returned.

If an access request is approved or denied, it will **no longer** be included in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of access requests.

put.summary: Request access to a board
put.description: Request access to a board.

schema.description: Board access request data.

comment.description: An optional comment provided by the user requesting access, explaining why they need access to the board.

204.description: The access request has been sent.

post.summary: Process a list of access requests
post.description: Process a list of access requests.

schema.description: Board access requests data.

requests_to_approve.description: A list of board access requests to approve.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board for which the access request is being processed.

user_id.description: The ID of the user who is requesting access to the board.

role_id.description: The ID of the role that will be assigned to the user once their access request is approved.

requests_to_deny.description: A list of board access requests to deny.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board for which the access request is being processed.

user_id.description: The ID of the user who is requesting access to the board.

reason.description: An optional reason for denying the access request, which can be provided by the user processing the request.

204.description: The access requests have been processed.

get.summary: Get a list of the BAI prompts for a board
get.description:
Returns a list of all Businessmap AI (BAI) prompts for a board.

There are **three** types of prompts:

- **Comments and Emails** - Prompts that allow the generation of text content for a comment or email 
- **Subtasks** -  Prompts that generate text for comments or email content associated with an existing card.
- **Summaries** - Prompts that generate a concise summary of a card's contents, such as its title, description, and other relevant details.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of BAI prompts.

post.summary: Add a BAI prompt to a board
post.description: Add a Businessmap AI (BAI) prompt to a board.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get the details of a BAI prompt for a board
get.description: Get the details of a Businessmap AI (BAI) prompt for a board.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

patch.summary: Update a BAI prompt for a board
patch.description: Update a Businessmap AI (BAI) for a board. The **type** of a prompt **cannot** be changed once it's created.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Remove a BAI prompt from a board
delete.description: Remove a Businessmap AI (BAI) from a board.

204.description: The Businessmap AI (BAI) prompt has been removed.

get.summary: Get a list of board assignees
get.description:
Get a list of user IDs assigned to a specific board, along with their respective roles.

You can get a list of all roles by calling the [roles endpoint](#/operations/getRoles#Request) and a list of all users by calling the [users endpoint](#/operations/getUsers#request).

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of user IDs assigned to a specific board, along with their respective roles.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user assigned to the board.

role_id.description: The unique identifier of the role assigned to the user on the board.

get.description: Returns the role of a user assigned to a specific board.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.title: Board user role
data.description: A role id.

role_id.description: The unique identifier of the role assigned to the user on the board.

put.summary: Assign a user to a board
put.description: Assign a user to a board or update their role.

schema.description: Role data.

role_id.description: The unique identifier of the role to be assigned to the user on the board.

204.description: The user's role was successfully assigned or updated.

delete.summary: Unassign a user from a board
delete.description:
Unassign a user from a board.

<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>
> This action only removes the user's assignment and does **not** delete the user themselves.

204.description: The user has been successfully unassigned from the board.

users_to_add_or_update.description: A list of user IDs and their roles to be added to or updated on the board.

user_ids_to_remove.description: A list of user IDs to be unassigned from the board.

204.description: The users have been assigned, unassigned, or had their roles updated successfully.

get.summary: Get the specified board's workflows and their effective cycle time columns
get.description:
Get the specified board's workflows and their effective cycle time columns.

By default, each board has two workflows:
- **Cards workflow**
- **Initiatives workflow**

Each of these workflows includes the following cycle time columns by default:
- **Requested**
- **In Progress**

You can find a list of all boards by calling the [boards endpoint](#/operations/getBoards#Request).

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of workflows and their effective cycle time columns.

get.summary: Get a list of the allowed values of a custom field of type dropdown on a specified board
get.description:
Get a list of allowed values for a custom field of type **dropdown** on the specified board.  You can obtain detailed information about the allowed values by calling the [allowedValues endpoint](#/operations/getCustomFieldAllowedValues#Request).

By default the allowed values that are added on a **board** level **match** the ones that are on a **global** level.  

If a **new** allowed value is added on a **board** level, the allowed value will **also** appear on a **global** level.  
If a custom field is **already** added to a board, any additional allowed values added on a **global** level will **not** automatically appear on a **board** level.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of allowed values.

get.summary: Get a single allowed value for a custom field of type dropdown on a specified board
get.description: Get a single allowed value for a custom field of type **dropdown** on the specified board.  You can obtain detailed information about the allowed value by calling the [allowedValues endpoint](#/operations/getCustomFieldAllowedValue#Request).

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

put.summary: Add or update an allowed value for a custom field of type dropdown on a specified board
put.description: Add or update an allowed value for a custom field of type **dropdown** on the specified board. If a **new** allowed value is added on a **board** level, the allowed value will **also** appear on a **global** level.

schema.description: Board custom field allowed value data.

position.description: The position of the allowed value. `0` is the first position. If no position is specified in the request body, the allowed value will be added to the **end** of the list.

is_default.description: Controls whether the allowed value is a default value for the custom field on the specified board.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

patch.summary: Update an allowed value for a custom field of type dropdown on a specified board
patch.description: Update an allowed value for a custom field of type **dropdown** on a specified board.

schema.description: Board custom field allowed value data.

position.description: The position of the allowed value. `0` is the first position. If no position is specified in the request body, the allowed value will be added to the **end** of the list.

is_default.description: Controls whether the allowed value is a default value for the custom field on the specified board.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Remove an allowed value for a custom field of type dropdown on a specified board
delete.description:
Remove an allowed value for a custom field of type **dropdown** on a specified board.

<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>
> This action only removes the allowed value from the board and does **not** delete the allowed value itself.

204.description: The allowed value has been removed.

get.summary: Get a custom field available on a specified board
get.description:
Get a custom field available on a specified board.
<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>The response body of this request may contain mostly null values, if the custom field hasn't been updated **at least once** on a board level.
>In those cases you can call the [effectiveSettings endpoint](#/operations/getBoardCustomFieldEffectiveSettings#Request).

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

put.summary: Add a custom field to a board
put.description:
Add a custom field to a board. When adding a custom field to a board, please check its **availability level** in the **global** settings using the [customFields/{field_id} endpoint](#/operations/getCustomField#Request).

When set to `0` the custom field has to be added to boards **manually**.

When set to `1` the custom field is added **automatically** to **all new** boards and will **not** be added to **existing** boards.

When set to `2` the custom field is added **automatically** to **all** boards and **cannot** be removed.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

patch.summary: Update the properties of a custom field available on a specified board
patch.description: Update the properties of a custom field available on a specified board.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Make a custom field unavailable on a specified board
delete.description: Make a custom field unavailable on a specified board.

204.description: The custom field is no longer available on the board.

get.summary: Get the effective settings of a custom field available on a specified board
get.description: Get the effective settings of a custom field available on a specified board. If the custom field has **not** been updated on a **board** level, the response body of this request will contain the default values inherited from the **global** settings found in the [customFields/{field_id} endpoint](#/operations/getCustomField#Request).

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

dropdown_value_id.description: If the data field is a `dropdown`, this parameter specifies the ID of the value.

contributor_user_id.description: The ID of an existing user used if the data field is of type `contributor`.

dropdown_value_reference.description: This can be used to identify a dropdown value that is being created in the same request as the data field is being added or updated. This is only applicable when the data field is of type `dropdown`.

get.summary: Get the current board structure
get.description:
Retrieves the complete and current structural configuration of a specific board, including all **workflows**, **lanes**, and **columns**, each uniquely identified by an ID. A board is composed of one or more **workflows**, which are the top-level organizational units. Each workflow contains horizontal **lanes** and vertical **columns**. **Lanes** are horizontal swimlanes that span across multiple **columns**. **Columns** are vertical sections that define process stages. Every board is initialized with two default workflows: the **Initiatives Workflow** and the **Cards Workflow**. Each of these starts with its own default lane - the **Portfolio Lane** for Initiatives and the **Default Swimlane** for Cards - and a set of default columns: **Backlog**, **Requested**, **In Progress**, **Done**, and **Ready to Archive**. There are **three** available **workflow types**: **Cards**, **Initiatives**, and **Timeline**.

Related endpoints:

- [Workflows](#/operations/getWorkflows#Request) - [Lanes](#/operations/getLanes#Request) - [Columns](#/operations/getColumns#Request)

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get a list of standard fields visible on the specified board
get.description:
Returns a list of standard fields that are visible for each card on the specified board.  
These fields control what metadata is displayed directly on cards in the **UI**, such as:

- **`last_modified`** – Shows the date and time the card was last updated.
- **`in_current_position_since`** – Shows how long the card has been in its current position.
- **`created_at`** – Displays when the card was originally created.
- **`reporter`** – Shows the name of the user who created the card.

All standard visible fields are **disabled by default** and must be explicitly enabled per board via **PUT** request to the [visibleStandardFields/{field_name} endpoint](/operations/addBoardStandardField#Request).

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board.

last_modified.description: Determines whether each card on the board shows the date and time when it was last modified in the **UI**.

in_current_position_since.description: Determines whether each card on the board shows the date and time when it was moved to its current position in the **UI**.

created_at.description: Determines whether each card on the board shows the date and time when it was created in the **UI**.

reporter.description: Determines whether each card on the board shows the name of the user who created it in the **UI**.

get.summary: Check if a standard field is visible on the specified board
get.description: Check if a standard field is visible on the specified board.

204.description: The standard field is visible on the board.

put.summary: Make a standard field visible on the specified board
put.description: Make a standard field visible on the specified board.

204.description: The standard field is now visible on the board.

delete.summary: Make a standard field no longer visible on the specified board
delete.description: Make a standard field no longer visible on the specified board.

204.description: The standard field is no longer visible on the board.

get.summary: Get a list of boards
get.description:
Retrieves a list of boards with their relevant details, optionally filtered using various parameters.

There are two types of boards:

  - **Team Kanban Board** - Allows your team to visualize, track, and manage their work in a single place. It helps keep the team aligned and organized while working on tasks and goals together.
  - **AI Canvas** - Integrates insights, tasks, and collaboration from multiple sources. It operates intelligently across connected boards or systems, enabling proactive project or product management with real-time, AI-driven guidance.

parameters.name: board_ids
parameters.description: Filters results to include only boards with matching IDs.

parameters.name: workspace_ids
parameters.description: Filters results to include only boards belonging to the specified workspace IDs.

parameters.name: is_archived
parameters.description: Set to `0` to retrieve only non-archived boards, or `1` to retrieve only archived boards.

parameters.name: if_assigned
parameters.description: Set to `1` to retrieve only boards to which you are assigned.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: A list of fields to include in the response.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties to expand for additional details in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of boards.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board.

name.description: The name of the board.

description.description: The description of the board.

type.description: The type of the board. A value of `1` indicates a **Kanban board**, while a value of `2` indicates an **AI Canvas**.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board.

main_maestro_instance_id.description: The unique identifier of the primary Whiteboard for the board. Applicable **only** to **AI Canvas** boards and automatically generated upon their creation.

Get the details of a single board.

You can further refine the response by adding query parameters such as `fields` to return only specific attributes, or `expand` to include additional details about related resources.

There is an account-wide setting that controls whether attachments are enabled or disabled for all users. This setting also allows administrators to specify the maximum allowed file size for attachments, which can be set to any value between 1 MB and 30 MB.

The status of this setting can also be checked or modified programmatically via the [getCompanySetting endpoint](#/operations/getCompanySetting) and providing the parameter `features.card_attachments.disabled` or `features.card_attachments.max_size`.

put.summary: Set the card's cover image
put.description:
Set an attached image file as the card's cover image.

Only users with both “Edit card details” and “Card attachments” permissions can make and remove card covers.

Only attached image files in the formats **jpg**, **jpeg**, **png**, and **webp** can be set as cover images. Additionally, the attachment for the cover image cannot be uploaded from Google Drive and One Drive.

The maximum width and height for covers is 210x210 pixels. If the uploaded image is larger than that, the system will crop it to that size.

schema.description: The link of the cover image

cover_image_link.description: The link to the attached image file to be used as a cover image. For files uploaded through the application UI, this link is represented as a path that starts with `/files/` followed by a unique string identifier for the file.

204.description: The cover image has been set.

delete.description:
Remove the cover image previously set for the card.

<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>
> This action does **not** delete the image file, it only unsets it as a cover image for the current card. The file remains as an attachment on the card.

204.description: The card's cover image has been removed.

get.summary: Get a list of parent cards

put.summary: Make a card a parent of a given card
put.description: Establishes a parent-child relationship between two cards by designating one card as the parent of a specified card. This operation also allows specifying the positions of the cards in each other's list of linked cards.

schema.description: Link data.

linked_card_position.description: The position of the parent card within the child's list of linked cards.

delete.description: Remove the parent-child link between two cards, effectively making them independent of each other.

parameters.name: exceeding_reason
parameters.description: The reason for exceeding the specified limit. This query parameter can be appended to the request URL to filter cards with a specific exceeding reason.

204.description: The two cards are no longer a child and a parent.

parameters.name: parent_card_id
parameters.description: A parent card id.

post.description: Add a sticker to a card. A single sticker can be added **multiple** times by default. If you want to prevent adding a sticker that has already been added to the card, set the `if_not_present` parameter to `1`. delete.description: Remove a sticker from a card.

get.summary: Get a list of card templates
get.description: Retrieves a list of card templates with their relevant details, optionally filtered using various parameters.

parameters.name: template_ids
parameters.description: Filters results to include only templates with matching IDs.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filters the results to include only templates that match the specified name.

parameters.name: availability
parameters.description:
A list of the availability levels by which to filter the card templates.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the card template can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the card template is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the card template is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a card template's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

parameters.name: is_enabled
parameters.description: Filters card templates based on whether they are enabled.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: A list of fields to include in the response.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties to expand for additional details in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of card templates.

post.description:
Creates a new card template.

Each template always contains a **primary card**, represented by the `primary_card` object, which holds the data used to create the template.

Templates can also include multiple **additional cards** linked to the primary card, e.g. child, relative, etc. These are represented in the `other_cards` array.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get the details of a single card template
get.description:
Get the details of a single card template.
<!-- theme: info -->

> #### Info
>
> You can use the [GET /cardTemplates](#/operations/getCardTemplates) endpoint with the `template_ids` query parameter to retrieve multiple card templates in a single request. You can further refine the response by adding query parameters such as `fields` to return only specific attributes, or `expand` to include additional details about related resources.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

patch.summary: Update a card template
patch.description: Update a card template.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Delete a card template
delete.description: Delete a card template.

204.description: The card template has been deleted.

get.summary: Get a list of card templates creation, deletion and update events
get.description: Get a list of events related to the creation, deletion, and updates of card templates. The results can be filtered using optional criteria.

parameters.name: template_ids
parameters.description: A list of template IDs to filter the history results.

parameters.name: user_ids
parameters.description: A list of user IDs who performed the actions.

parameters.name: event_types
parameters.description: The types of actions performed on the card templates.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The first date and time from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The last date and time from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: The first date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: The last date from which to retrieve results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Results are **always** paginated and returned in pages. This parameter controls which page is returned. Defaults to the **first** page if not specified.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page. Defaults to `100` and has a maximum value of `200`.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of card template events.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

all_pages.description: The total number of pages in the result set.

current_page.description: The current page number.

results_per_page.description: The number of results per page.

data.description: A list of events related to card templates.

post.description: Schedule multiple card templates for deletion in a single request. Card templates are not deleted immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the deletion runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Card template IDs data.

template_ids.description: A list of card template IDs to be deleted.

204.description: The card templates have been scheduled for deletion. The deletion runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

post.description: Schedule multiple card templates for disabling in a single request. Card templates are not disabled immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the disabling runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Card template IDs data.

template_ids.description: A list of card template IDs to be disabled.

204.description: The card templates have been scheduled for disabling. The operation runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

post.description: Schedule multiple card templates for enabling in a single request. Card templates are not enabled immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the enabling runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Card template IDs data.

template_ids.description: A list of card template IDs to be enabled.

204.description: The card templates have been scheduled for enabling. The operation runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

The availability level of the card type.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the card type can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the card type is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a card type's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

 You can see a list of all cards in the [cards](#operations/getCards#Request) endpoint.

delete.description:

 You can see a list of all cards in the [cards](#operations/getCards#Request) endpoint.

post.description:
Cards always require the lane and column of the board where they will be created. For more information about a board's structure, please see the **[Current structure](#/paths/boards-board_id--currentStructure/get#Requestt)** endpoint.

Most cards are initially placed in the **Requested** column of a specific lane, depending on the workflow. Standard cards are typically created in the **Cards Workflow**, while initiatives belong to the **Initiatives Workflow**.

Cards can also be created in a **Timeline Workflow**, depending on the use case and board setup.
<!-- theme: info -->

> #### Info
>
> If you'd like to create multiple cards at once, you can use the [/cards/createMany](#/operations/createManyCards) endpoint to create several cards at once.
>
> This bulk creation method helps you reduce network overhead, speed up your workflows, and improve overall performance by batching operations into a single API call.

exceeding_reason.description: The reason for exceeding a limit, such as the maximum number of cards in a lane or column.

reporter_user_id.description: The user ID of the reporter if the card is created on behalf of someone else.

reporter_email.description: The email of the reporter if the card is created on behalf of someone else without a known user ID.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of cards.

get.summary: Get a hierarchical graph of a card's children
get.description:
Retrieves the hierarchical structure of a specified card's child relationships in the form of a graph.
A card can have one or more direct children (Level 1), and each child can have its own children (Level 2).
The `depth` parameter indicates the level of the child in the hierarchy, relative to the original parent card:
- `1` - Direct child of the specified card.
- `2` - Child of a Level 1 card.
- `3` - Child of a Level 2 card, and so on.
