get.summary: Get the details of a specific column
get.description:
Get the details of a specific column on a board.

 You can see a list of all boards in the [boards](#operations/getBoards#Request) endpoint.

 You can see a list of all columns in a board in the [columns](#operations/getColumns#Request) endpoint.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the details of the specified column.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

patch.summary: Update a column
patch.description:
Update the details of a specific column

 You can see a list of all boards in the [boards](#operations/getBoards#Request) endpoint.

 You can see a list of all columns in a board in the [columns](#operations/getColumns#Request) endpoint.

 You can see a list of all workflows in a board in the [workflows](#operations/getWorkflows#Request) endpoint.

schema.description: Data required to update a column.

oneOf.title: Update main column
oneOf.description: Data to update a main column.

section.description:
The section of the workflow where the column is located. Valid values are:
 - `1` - Backlog
 - `2` - Requested
 - `3` - Progress
 - `4` - Done
 Adding columns to the **Archive area**, section `5`, is not allowed.

oneOf.title: Update subcolumn
oneOf.description: Data to update a subcolumn.

parent_column_id.description: The ID of the parent column, if the column is part of a hierarchical structure.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Delete a column from a board
delete.description: Delete a specific column from a board. Optionally, you can specify another column to move the cards and/or cycle time data from the deleted column. If no column is specified for the cycle time data, it will be discarded.

parameters.name: move_cards_to_column_id
parameters.description: The ID of the column to which the cards from the deleted column should be moved, if any.

parameters.name: move_metrics_to_column_id
parameters.description: The ID of the column to which the cycle time data from the deleted column should be moved, if any. If not provided or set to `null`, the cycle time data will be discarded.

204.description: The column has been successfully deleted.

get.summary: Get a list of all company settings and their values
get.description: Get a list of all company settings and their values.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of settings.

get.summary: Get the details of a company setting and its current value
get.description: Get the details of a company setting and its current value.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

put.summary: Set the value of a company setting
put.description: Set the value of a company setting.

schema.description: Represents the setting's value, which can be either an `integer` or a `string`.

value.description: The value of the setting.

204.description: The setting's value was successfully updated.

delete.summary: Remove the value of a company setting
delete.description:
Removes the value of a company setting and sets it to `null`. When a company setting is `null`, it behaves according to its default value.

After removing a setting, the [companySettings endpoint](#/operations/getCompanySettings#Request) will return the default value of the setting, while the [companySetting/{setting_name} endpoint](#/operations/getCompanySetting#Request) will return `null`.

204.description: The setting's value has been set to `null` and reverted to its default value.

get.summary: Get a list of the allowed values for a custom field of type dropdown
get.description:
Get a list of the allowed values for a custom field of type `dropdown`.

 You can see a list of all custom fields in the [customFields](#operations/getCustomFields#Request) endpoint.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of allowed values for the specified custom field.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of allowed values for the `dropdown` custom field.

value_id.description: The ID of the allowed value.

post.summary: Create a new allowed value for a dropdown custom field
post.description:
Add a new allowed value to a dropdown custom field.

You can see a list of all custom fields in the [customFields](#operations/getCustomFields#Request) endpoint.
<!-- theme: info -->

> #### Info
>
> If the allowed value is created at the **global** level after the custom field has already been assigned to a board, it will not appear on the existing board. **Globally** added allowed values will only be applied to boards after the custom field is assigned to them.

schema.description: Data to create a new allowed value for a dropdown custom field.

position.description: The position of the value within the dropdown list. `0` represents the first position.

is_enabled.description:
Indicates whether the value is enabled.
 - `0`: Not enabled
 - `1`: Enabled

is_default.description:
Indicates whether the value is selected by default.
 - `0`: Not default
 - `1`: Default

value.description: The actual value to be added to the dropdown.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

value_id.description: The ID of the created allowed value.

get.summary: Get the details of a specific allowed value for a dropdown custom field
get.description:
Get the details of a specific allowed value for a `dropdown` custom field.

 You can see a list of all custom fields in the [customFields](#operations/getCustomFields#Request) endpoint.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the details of the specified allowed value.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

patch.summary: Update an allowed value
patch.description:
Update the properties of a specific allowed value for a `dropdown` custom field.

 You can see a list of all custom fields in the [customFields](#operations/getCustomFields#Request) endpoint.

schema.description: Data to update an allowed value for a dropdown custom field.

position.description: The position of the value within the dropdown list. `0` represents the first position.

is_enabled.description:
Indicates whether the value is enabled.
 - `0`: Not enabled
 - `1`: Enabled

is_default.description:
Indicates whether the value is selected by default.
 - `0`: Not default
 - `1`: Default

value.description: The updated value for the dropdown option.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Delete an allowed value from a dropdown custom field
delete.description:
Delete a specific allowed value from a dropdown custom field.

 Optionally, you can specify another value to replace the deleted value on cards where it is currently used. If no replacement value is provided, the deleted value will simply be removed.

 You can see a list of all custom fields in the [customFields](#operations/getCustomFields#Request) endpoint.

parameters.name: replace_with_value_id
parameters.description: The ID of the value to replace the deleted value on cards where it is currently used. If not provided, the value will be removed without replacement.

204.description: The allowed value has been successfully deleted.

post.summary: Manage allowed values for a dropdown custom field
post.description:
Perform batch operations to create, update, or delete allowed values for a dropdown custom field.

You can see a list of all custom fields in the [customFields](#operations/getCustomFields#Request) endpoint.
<!-- theme: info -->

> #### Info
>
> If the allowed value is created at the **global** level after the custom field has already been assigned to a board, it will not appear on the existing board. **Globally** added allowed values will only be applied to boards after the custom field is assigned to them.

schema.description: Data to manage allowed values for a dropdown custom field.

200.description: Successfully performed the batch operations on allowed values.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get a list of the boards where the specified custom field is available
get.description:
Retrieve a list of the boards where the specified custom field is available, including the properties of the field on each board.

If the values of the custom field's properties are set to `null`, it means that no changes were done on a board level, and the properties are set to their default values. To check the default values of the custom field properties, you can call the [customFields/{field_id} endpoint](#operations/getCustomFields#Request) endpoint.

You can see a list of all custom fields in the [customFields](#operations/getCustomFields#Request) endpoint.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of board custom field properties.

post.summary: Change the availability of a custom field on one or more boards
post.description:
Change the availability of a custom field on one or more boards.

The response body **will not** include boards from which the custom field is removed, even if you attempt to remove it from a board where it is **already unavailable**
<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>
> If the request body contains only `board_ids_to_remove`, the response will contain an **empty array**.

schema.description: Custom field boards data.

boards_to_add_or_update.description: A list of board IDs where the custom field should be made available or its availability updated.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board where the custom field should be made available or have its availability updated.

color.description: The color of the custom field on a board level in hexadecimal format.

is_always_present.description: Controls whether the custom field must always be present on all cards on the board.

position.description: The position of the custom field on a board level. `0` is the first position. If you don't specify a position, the custom field will be added to the **end** of the list.

board_ids_to_remove.description: A list of board IDs where the custom field should be made unavailable.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get a list of cards with a specific custom field and their values
get.description:
Get a list of cards to which a specific custom field has been added, along with the associated values for that field.

 You can see a list of all custom fields in the [customFields](#operations/getCustomFields#Request) endpoint.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of cards and their custom field values.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of custom field values for the cards.

card_id.description: The ID of the card.

get.summary: Get a list of custom field management history events.
get.description: Get a list of custom field management history events.

parameters.name: field_ids
parameters.description: A list of IDs of the custom fields for which you want to retrieve the history.

parameters.name: event_types
parameters.description:
The types of actions performed on the custom fields. Possible values include:
 - `field_created`: Custom field was created
 - `field_updated`: Custom field was updated
 - `field_disabled`: Custom field was disabled
 - `field_enabled`: Custom field was enabled
 - `field_deleted`: Custom field was deleted
 - `field_added_to_board`: Custom field was added to a board
 - `field_updated_for_board`: Custom field was updated for a board
 - `field_removed_from_board`: Custom field was removed from a board
 - `allowed_value_created`: Allowed value was created
 - `allowed_value_updated`: Allowed value was updated
 - `allowed_value_deleted`: Allowed value was deleted
 - `allowed_value_disabled`: Allowed value was disabled
 - `allowed_value_enabled`: Allowed value was enabled
 - `allowed_value_added_to_board`: Allowed value was added to a board
 - `allowed_value_updated_for_board`: Allowed value was updated for a board
 - `allowed_value_removed_from_board`: Allowed value was removed from a board
 - `default_contributor_added_to_board`: Default contributor was added to a board
 - `default_contributor_removed_from_board`: Default contributor was removed from a board

parameters.name: user_ids
parameters.description: A list of user IDs who performed the actions.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The starting date and time for retrieving results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The ending date and time for retrieving results, in `YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss` format.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: The starting date for retrieving results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: The ending date for retrieving results, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to retrieve. Defaults to the **first** page if not specified. Results are always paginated.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results to return per page. Defaults to `100`, with a maximum of `200`.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of custom field management history events.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

all_pages.description: The total number of pages in the paginated result set.

current_page.description: The page number of the current set of results.

results_per_page.description: The number of results per page.

data.description: A list of custom field management history events.

get.summary: Get a list of custom fields
get.description:
Retrieves a list of custom fields with their relevant details, optionally filtered using various parameters.
The available custom field types are:
- `single_line_text` – A single-line text input.
- `multi_line_text` – A multi-line text input for longer content.
- `number` – A numeric value.
- `date` – A calendar date. It can include a time component, if `include_time` is enabled.
- `link` – A valid URL, typically used to reference external resources.
- `dropdown` – A selection from a predefined list of options.
- `contributor` – One or more users assigned as contributors.
- `file` – A file upload field.
- `vote` – A voting-style field for gathering preferences.
- `card_picker` – A field for selecting a card reference based on the specified filters.
- `calculated_number` – A number field with a value calculated from card fields (which can include other custom fields) or manually input values.
- `calculated_date` – A date field with a value calculated from card fields (which can include other custom fields) or manually input values.

parameters.name: field_ids
parameters.description: A list of custom field IDs used to filter the returned custom fields.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filters custom fields by their exact name.

parameters.name: availability
parameters.description:
A list of the availability levels by which to filter the custom fields.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the custom field can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the custom field is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the custom field is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a custom field's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

parameters.name: is_enabled
parameters.description: Filters custom fields based on whether they are enabled.

parameters.name: is_immutable
parameters.description: Filters custom fields based on whether they are immutable, i.e. once the custom field is added to a card and a value is set, that value can't be changed anymore.

parameters.name: is_always_present
parameters.description: Filters custom fields based on whether they are always present, i.e. they must be present on all cards on the board to which the custom field is added.

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: A list of the types of custom fields you want to retrieve.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties for which additional details should be included in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of custom fields.

post.description: Create a new custom field. In order to add a custom field to one or more boards you can use the [customFields/{field_id}/batchBoardOperations endpoint](#/operations/updateCustomFieldBoards).

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get the details of the specified custom field
get.description:
Get the details of the specified custom field.
<!-- theme: info -->

> #### Info
>
> You can use the [GET /customFields](#/operations/getCustomFields) endpoint with the `field_ids` query parameter to retrieve multiple custom fields in a single request. You can further refine the response by adding query parameters such as `fields` to return only specific attributes, or `expand` to include additional details about related resources.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

patch.summary: Update a custom field
patch.description: Update a custom field.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Delete a custom field
delete.description:
Delete a custom field.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> ### Warning
>
> This will remove the custom field from **all boards and cards** associated with it. All information entered in the field will be **lost** as well. This action **cannot** be reversed.

204.description: The custom field has been deleted.

post.description: Schedule multiple custom fields for deletion in a single request. Custom fields are not deleted immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the deletion runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Custom field IDs data.

field_ids.description: A list of custom field IDs to be deleted.

204.description: The custom fields have been scheduled for deletion. The deletion runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

post.description: Schedule multiple custom fields for disabling in a single request. Custom fields are not disabled immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the disabling runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Custom field IDs data.

field_ids.description: A list of custom field IDs to be disabled.

204.description: The custom fields have been scheduled for disabling. The operation runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

post.description: Schedule multiple custom fields for enabling in a single request. Custom fields are not enabled immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the enabling runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Custom field IDs data.

field_ids.description: A list of custom field IDs to be enabled.

204.description: The custom fields have been scheduled for enabling. The operation runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

post.description: Schedule multiple custom field categories for deletion in a single request. Custom field categories are not deleted immediately — they are queued for batch processing and the deletion runs in the background. There is no way to estimate how long the process will take.

schema.description: Custom field category IDs data.

category_ids.description: A list of custom field category IDs to be deleted.

204.description: The custom field categories have been scheduled for deletion. The deletion runs in the background as a batch job and may take some time to complete.

get.summary: Get a list of data fields
get.description:
Retrieves a list of data fields with their relevant details, optionally filtered using various parameters.
The available data field types are:
- `single_line_text` – A single-line text input.
- `number` – A numeric value.
- `date` – A calendar date.
- `dropdown` – A selection from a predefined list of options.
- `contributor` – One user assigned as contributor.
- `calculated_number` – A number field with manually input values for boards and calculated by a formula type for workspaces based on board values.

parameters.name: data_field_ids
parameters.description: A list of data field IDs used to filter the returned data fields.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Find a data field by its exact name.

parameters.name: is_enabled
parameters.description: Filters data fields based on whether they are enabled.

parameters.name: types
parameters.description: A list of the types of data fields you want to retrieve.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties for which additional details should be included in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of data fields.

post.description:
Create a new data field.

In order to add a data field to one or more boards you can use the [dataFields/{data_field_id}/batchBoardOperations endpoint](#/operations/updateDataFieldBoards) and to add a data field to one or more workspaces you can use the [dataFields/{data_field_id}/batchWorkspaceOperations endpoint](#/operations/updateDataFieldWorkspaces).

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get the details of a data field
get.description: Get the details of the specified data field.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties for which additional details should be included in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of data field data with potential data for boards, workspaces and allowed_values.

name.description: The name of the data field.

description.description: The description of the data field.

color.description: The color of the data field. This is a hex color code.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the data field is enabled.

boards.description: A list of boards where the data field is available. This is only returned if the `boards` value is specified in the `expand` query parameter.

board_id.description: The ID of the board where the data field is available.

value.description: The value of the data field on the board.

baseline.description: The baseline value of the data field on the board.

value_is_locked.description: Indicates whether the value is locked on the board.

dropdown_value_id.description: If the data field is a `dropdown`, this parameter specifies the ID of the value.

contributor_user_id.description: The ID of an existing user used if the data field is of type `contributor`.

workspaces.description: A list of workspaces where the data field is available. This is only returned if the `workspaces` value is specified in the `expand` query parameter.

value_is_locked_for_all_boards_in_worksp.description: A list of workspaces IDs where the data field has a locked value for all boards in the workspace.

apply_to_all_boards_in_workspace_ids.description: A list of workspaces IDs where the data field is enabled for all boards in the workspace.

workspace_data_fields.description: A list of workspaces where the data field is available.

workspace_id.description: The ID of the workspace where the data field is available.

value.description: The value of the data field on the workspace.

baseline.description: The baseline value of the data field on the workspace.

show_in_dashboard.description: Indicates whether to show the data field in the dashboard.

dropdown_value_id.description: If the data field is a dropdown, this parameter specifies the ID of the value.

contributor_user_id.description: The ID of an existing user used if the data field is of type contributor.

patch.summary: Update a data field
patch.description: Update a data field.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Delete a data field
delete.description:
Delete a data field.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> ### Warning
>
> This will remove the data field from **all boards and workspaces** associated with it. All information entered in the field will be **lost** as well. This action **cannot** be reversed.

204.description: The data field has been deleted.

get.summary: Get a list of docs
get.description: Retrieve a list of all docs on an account which the user can access. The results can be filtered using optional criteria.

parameters.name: doc_ids
parameters.description: Get a list of details for specific docs by their IDs.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Filters results to include only docs that match the specified title.

parameters.name: is_archived
parameters.description: Set to `1` to return only archived docs, or `0` to return only non-archived docs.

parameters.name: show_in_main_doc_list
parameters.description: Set to `1` to return only docs that are shown in the main doc list, or `0` to return only docs that are hidden from the main doc list.

parameters.name: is_important
parameters.description: Set to `1` to return only important docs, or `0` to return only non-important docs.

parameters.name: for_welcome
parameters.description: Set to `1` to return only docs shown on the welcome screen, or `0` to exclude them.

parameters.name: is_indexed_for_ai
parameters.description: Set to `1` to return only docs indexed for AI, or `0` to return only non-indexed docs.

parameters.name: parent_doc_ids
parameters.description: A list of parent doc IDs. Returns docs that have any of the specified parent doc IDs.

parameters.name: show_predecessors_of_accessible_docs
parameters.description: Set to `1` to include predecessor docs of accessible docs in the results.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties to expand for additional details in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

doc_id.description: The unique identifier of the doc.

parent_doc_id.description: The ID of the parent doc, if any.

is_archived.description: Indicates whether the doc is archived.

is_important.description: Indicates whether the doc is marked as important.

position.description: The position of the doc in its parent's list.

show_in_main_doc_list.description: Indicates whether the doc is shown in the main doc list.

updated_at.description: The date and time when the doc was last updated.

author_id.description: The ID of the user who created the doc.

size.description: The size of the doc in bytes.

fully_hidden_docs.description: A list of fully hidden docs. This is only returned if `fully_hidden_docs` is specified in the `expand` query parameter.

docs_without_owners.description: A list of docs without owners. This is only returned if `docs_without_owners` is specified in the `expand` query parameter.

post.description: Create a new doc.

is_important.description: Set to `1` to mark the doc as important, or `0` otherwise.

for_welcome.description: Set to `1` to show the doc on the welcome screen, or `0` otherwise.

is_archived.description: Set to `1` to create the doc as archived, or `0` otherwise.

is_indexed_for_ai.description: Set to `1` to index the doc for AI, or `0` otherwise. Omit to use account default.

parent_doc_id.description: The ID of the parent doc, if this doc should be a child of another doc.

position.description: The position of the doc in its parent's list.

show_in_main_doc_list.description: Set to `1` to show the doc in the main doc list, or `0` to hide it.

example.title: My New Document
example.content: This is the content of my document.

delete.summary: Delete a doc
delete.description:
Delete a doc. The user must be an owner of the doc.
First-level docs that are shown in the main doc list may require doc manager permission to delete.

everyone_can_view.description: Indicates whether everyone can view the doc.

post.summary: Add, update or remove a data field on one or more boards
post.description:
Add, update or remove a data field on one or more boards.

The response body **will not** include boards from which the data field is removed, even if you attempt to remove it from a board where it is **already unavailable**
<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>
> If the request body contains only `board_ids_to_remove`, the response will contain an **empty array**.

schema.description: Data field boards data.

boards_to_add_or_update.description: A list of board IDs where the data field should be made available or its availability updated.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board where the custom field should be made available or have its parameters updated.

value.description: The value to be set on the board. If the value is null it will set empty value.

baseline.description: The baseline value to be set on the board. If the baseline is null it will set empty value.

value_is_locked.description: Indicates whether the value is locked.

dropdown_value_id.description: If the data field is a `dropdown`, this parameter specifies the ID of the value to be set.

contributor_user_id.description: The ID of an existing user, used when the data field is of type `contributor`.

board_ids_to_remove.description: A list of board IDs where the data field should be made unavailable.

dropdown_value_id.description: If the data field is a `dropdown`, this parameter specifies the ID of the value to be set.

contributor_user_id.description: The ID of an existing user, used when the data field is of type `contributor`.

get.summary: Get a list of teams having access to a dashboard page
get.description: Retrieve a list of all teams that have access to the specified dashboard page, including their permissions and management roles.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of teams with access to the dashboard page.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of teams with their access and management roles for the dashboard page.

dashboard_page_id.description: The ID of the dashboard page.

can_edit.description:
Indicates if the team has permission to manage or edit the dashboard page
 - `0`: The team does not have permission to manage the dashboard page.
 - `1`: The team has permission to manage the dashboard page.

get.summary: Check if a team has access to a dashboard page
get.description:
Determine whether a specific team has access to the given dashboard page. Returns a `'204'` status code if the team has access, or a `404` if not.

 You can see a list of all teams in the [teams](#operations/getTeams#Request) endpoint.

204.description: The team has access to the dashboard page.

put.summary: Grant or update a team's access or manager role for a dashboard page
put.description:
Grant a team access to a dashboard page or update its management role. Use this endpoint to add a team to a dashboard page, or to set or remove its manager permissions.

 You can see a list of all teams in the [teams](#operations/getTeams#Request) endpoint.

schema.description: Optional request body for updating a team's management role for a dashboard page.

can_edit.description:
Indicates if the team can manage the dashboard page.
 - `0`: The team does not have permission to manage the dashboard page.
 - `1`: The team has permission to manage the dashboard page.

204.description: The team's access or manager role for the dashboard page has been successfully updated.

delete.summary: Remove a team's access to a dashboard page
delete.description:
Remove a team's access to the specified dashboard page. Use this endpoint to revoke all permissions for a team on a dashboard page.

 You can see a list of all teams in the [teams](#operations/getTeams#Request) endpoint.

204.description: The team has been removed and no longer has access to the dashboard page.

get.summary: Get a list of users who are assigned to the specified dashboard page.
get.description:
Retrieves a list of users who are assigned to the specified dashboard page.
There are two types of assigned users:
- **Dashboard members** – they can view the dashboard.
- **Dashboard managers** – they can add or remove users, workspaces, and widgets, as well as rename the dashboard.
If the `can_edit` property is set to `1`, the user is a **dashboard manager**. If it is set to `0`, the user is a **dashboard member**.

You can find a list of all users in the [users](#operations/getUsers#Request) endpoint.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of users assigned to the dashboard page.

dashboard_page_id.description: The unique identifier of the dashboard page.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user.

can_edit.description:
Indicates if the user can manage the dashboard page.

A value of `0` represents **Dashboard member**, meaning the user can view the dashboard.

A value of `1` represents **Dashboard manager**, meaning the user can add or remove users, workspaces, and widgets, as well as rename the dashboard.

get.summary: Check if a user is assigned to the specified dashboard page
get.description: Check if a user is assigned to the specified dashboard page.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.
schema.title: Dashboard page user

data.description: User dashboard page data

dashboard_page_id.description: The unique identifier of the dashboard page.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user.

A value of `1` represents **Dashboard manager**, meaning the user can add or remove users, workspaces, and widgets, as well as rename the dashboard.

get.summary: Get a list of dashboard pages
get.description:
Returns a list of dashboard pages available in the account.
You can optionally filter the results by specifying one or more dashboard page IDs.
You may also limit the fields returned for each dashboard page to only those you need (e.g., `dashboard_page_id`, `name`).

parameters.name: dashboard_page_ids
parameters.description:
Optional. A list of dashboard page IDs to filter the results.
Only dashboard pages with matching IDs will be returned.
If omitted, all dashboard pages will be included in the response.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Optional. A list of fields to include in the response for each dashboard page.
The allowed fields are: `dashboard_page_id` and `name`.
Use this parameter to reduce the amount of data returned if you only need specific properties.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of dashboard pages.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of dashboard pages.

delete.summary: Delete a dashboard page
delete.description:
Deletes the specified dashboard page, including all its settings, widgets, and access permissions.

 **Related Endpoints:**
 - All dashboard pages: [dashboard pages](#operations/getDashboardPages#Request).
 - All users associated with the dashboard page: [dashboard page users](#operations/getDashboardPageUsers#Request).
 - All teams associated with the dashboard page: [dashboard page teams](#operations/getDashboardPageTeams#Request).
 - All workspaces associated with the dashboard page: [dashboard page workspaces](#operations/getDashboardPageWorkspaces#Request).
 You can also clone the dashboard page before deleting it using the [clone dashboard page](#operations/cloneDashboardPage#Request) endpoint.

204.description: The dashboard page has been successfully deleted and is no longer accessible.

post.description:
Creates a copy of the specified dashboard page, including its settings, widgets, access permissions, and optionally its users, teams, and workspaces.

 **Related Endpoints:**
 - All dashboard pages: [dashboard pages](#operations/getDashboardPages#Request).
 - All users associated with the dashboard page: [dashboard page users](#operations/getDashboardPageUsers#Request).
 - All teams associated with the dashboard page: [dashboard page teams](#operations/getDashboardPageTeams#Request).
 - All workspaces associated with the dashboard page: [dashboard page workspaces](#operations/getDashboardPageWorkspaces#Request).

schema.description: Specifies which elements of the dashboard page should be included in the clone. All options default to `1` (enabled).

include_users.description:
If set to `1`, all users with access to the original dashboard page will also be granted access to the cloned page.
If set to `0`, only the user performing the clone operation will have access to the new page.

include_teams.description:
If set to `1`, all teams with access to the original dashboard page will also be granted access to the cloned page.
If set to `0`, team access will not be copied.

include_workspaces.description:
If set to `1`, all workspaces linked to the original dashboard page will also be linked to the cloned page.
If set to `0`, workspace associations will not be copied.

include_widgets.description:
If set to `1`, all widgets from the original dashboard page will be included in the cloned page.
If set to `0`, the cloned page will not contain any widgets.

200.description: The dashboard page was successfully cloned.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

get.summary: Get a list of discard reasons
get.description: Retrieves a list of discard reasons with their relevant details, optionally filtered using various parameters.

parameters.name: reason_ids
parameters.description: Filters results to include only discard reasons with matching IDs.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Filters discard reasons by their exact label (i.e. the name of the discard reason).

parameters.name: availability
parameters.description:
A list of the availability levels by which to filter the discard reasons.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the discard reason can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the discard reason is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the discard reason is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a discard reason's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

parameters.name: is_enabled
parameters.description: Filters discard reasons based on whether they are enabled.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: A list of fields to include in the response.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties to expand for additional details in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of discard reasons.

post.description: Create a new discard reason.

schema.description: Discard reason data.

availability.description:
The availability level of the discard reason.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the discard reason can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the discard reason is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the discard reason is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a discard reason's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the discard reason is enabled.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the discard reason is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the discard reason is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a discard reason's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

get.summary: Get all global privileges and their status for the specified user
get.description:
Get all global privileges and their status for the specified user. Global privileges represent **admin privileges** that grant elevated access across the entire account.
Available admin privileges include:
- `owner` - grants access to **all** other privileges.
- `create_team_workspaces` - allows creating and managing team workspaces within the account.
- `make_payments` - grants the user access to the **Payment plan** section.
- `manage_business_rules` - enables management and configuration of the **Business rules** section.
- `manage_integrations` - permits setup and management of the **Interagrations** section.
- `invite_users` - allows inviting new users to the account.
- `manage_teams` - grants the ability to create, update, and delete teams.
- `manage_user_wip_limits` - allows configuring Work-In-Progress limits for users.
- `manage_roles` - authorizes managing user roles and permissions.
- `manage_card_elements` - enables configuration and management of card elements, e.g. **Blockers**, **Stickers**, etc.
- `manage_security_settings` - grants the user access to the settings **Security & Audit** section. A user **additionally** needs the `access_audit_logs` privilege to view audit logs.
- `manage_dashboards` - allows creating and managing dashboards.
- `manage_logged_time` - grants rights to manage time logged on cards.
- `manage_worklog_configurations` - enables configuration of the **Worklog configurations** section.
- `access_audit_logs` - allows access to the **Audit logs** section in **Security & Audit**. If a user only has this privilege, they can **only** view audit logs and **cannot** manage security settings.
- `enable_and_disable_users` - permits enabling or disabling user accounts.
- `delete_users` - allows deletion of user accounts from the system.
- `manage_outcome_rules` - grants the user access to the **Outcome rules** section.
- `manage_data_fields`

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of global privileges.

get.summary: Check if the specified user is granted a particular global privilege
get.description: Check if the specified user is granted a particular global privilege. If a user isn't granted a particular global privilege, you'll receive a `404` response.

204.description: The user has the specified global privilege.

put.summary: Grant the specified user a particular global privilege
put.description:
Grant the specified user a particular global privilege.

<!-- theme: info -->
> ### Info
>
> If a user is granted the `owner` privilege, they automatically have all other privileges, even if the `available` parameter for those privileges shows a value of `0`.

204.description: The user is now granted the specified global privilege.

delete.summary: Remove a particular global privilege from the specified user
delete.description: Remove a particular global privilege from the specified user.

204.description: The user no longer has the specified global privilege.

get.summary: Get a list of all internal announcements
get.description:
Announcements are account-wide notifications that allow Owners to communicate important events and news to users within the account.

Retrieve a list of all internal announcements. You can filter the results by various parameters, such as announcement ids, active status, popover display, start and end times, and more.

parameters.name: announcement_ids
parameters.description: A list of internal announcement IDs to retrieve.

parameters.name: is_active
parameters.description: When set to 1 you will only get internal announcements which are started and not expired. When set to 0 you will get expired or announcements which are not yet started.

parameters.name: is_popover
parameters.description: When set to 1 you will only get internal announcements which are displayed as a popover.

parameters.name: start_time_from
parameters.description: The earliest start date and time from which to retrieve results.

parameters.name: start_time_to
parameters.description: The latest start date and time from which to retrieve results.

parameters.name: end_time_from
parameters.description: The earliest end date and time from which to retrieve results.

parameters.name: end_time_to
parameters.description: The latest end date and time from which to retrieve results.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: A list of fields to include in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of internal announcements.

post.description: Create a new internal announcement.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

delete.summary: Delete a lane
delete.description:
Delete a lane. This action cannot be undone.

If there are cards present in the lane, it will not be deleted until they're moved to another lane or discarded. You can specify a lane to which the cards will be moved using the `move_cards_to_lane_id` parameter.

parameters.name: move_cards_to_lane_id
parameters.description: The ID of a lane to which to move the cards from the deleted lane, if there are any.

parameters.name: move_metrics_to_lane_id
parameters.description: The ID of a lane to which to move the cycle time data associated with the deleted lane, if there is any. If this parameter is not provided or is null the cycle time data will be discarded.

204.description: The lane has been deleted.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all logged time categories.

parameters.name: category_ids
parameters.description: A list of category IDs to retrieve details for.

parameters.description: The label of the logged time category to filter by.

parameters.name: availability
parameters.description:
A list of the availability levels by which to filter the logged time categories.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the category can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the category is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the category is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a category's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

parameters.name: is_enabled
parameters.description: When set to 1, only enabled logged time categories are returned. When set to 0, only disabled categories are returned.

parameters.description: A list of fields to include in the response.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties for which you want to get additional details.

icon_id.description: An icon for the logged time category. If set to 0, the logged time category will not have an icon.

label.description: A label for the new logged time category.

description.description: An optional description of the new logged time category.

color.description: The color of the new logged time category in hexadecimal format.

availability.description:
The availability of the new category. 

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the category can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the category is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the category is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a category's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

is_enabled.description: Controls whether the category is enabled or not.

get.summary: Get a list of boards where the sticker is available
get.description: Retrieves a list of all boards on which the specified sticker is currently available.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of boards where the sticker is available.

data.description: A list of board IDs where the sticker is available.

board_id.description: The ID of the board.

post.summary: Make a sticker available or unavailable on multiple boards
post.description:
Add, update, or remove the availability of a sticker on several boards in a single request.
- Use `boardsToAddOrUpdate` to specify boards where the sticker should be available or update its limits.
- Use `boardIdsToRemove` to specify boards where the sticker should be made unavailable.

You can see a list of all boards where the sticker is available in the [sticker boards](#operations/getStickerBoards#Request) endpoint.

You can see a list of all boards in the [boards](#operations/getBoards#Request) endpoint.

schema.description: Object containing board assignment and limit data for the sticker.

boards_to_add_or_update.description:
An array of objects, each representing a board where you want the sticker to be available or update its limits.
Each object must include the `board_id` and may include `limit_per_board` and `limit_per_card` to restrict sticker usage.

board_id.description: The ID of the board.

limit_per_board.description: The maximum number of times the sticker can be used on the board (optional).

limit_per_card.description: The maximum number of times the sticker can be used on a single card (optional).

board_ids_to_remove.description: An array of board IDs where you want the sticker to be made unavailable.

items.description: The ID of the board to remove the sticker from.

200.description: Successfully updated the sticker's availability and limits on the specified boards.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

board_id.description: The ID of the board.

limit_per_board.description: The maximum number of times the sticker can be used on the board.

limit_per_card.description: The maximum number of times the sticker can be used on a single card.

items.description: An empty array if the sticker is unavailable on the specifed boards.

get.summary: Get a list of cards where the sticker is available
get.description:
Retrieves a list of all cards on which the specified sticker is currently available.

To get more details about a specific card, use the [cards/{card_id}](#/operations/getCard#Request) endpoint.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of cards where the sticker is available.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: An array of card objects, each representing a card where the sticker is available.

id.description: The ID of the sticker assignment on the card.

card_id.description: The ID of the card.

get.summary: Get a list of sticker creation, update, deletion, and board assignment events
get.description:
Returns a paginated list of sticker history events, including creation, update, enable/disable, deletion, and board assignment changes.
You can filter results by sticker IDs, user IDs, event types, and date ranges.

You can see a list of all stickers in the [stickers](#operations/getStickers#Request) endpoint.

You can see a list of all users in the [users](#operations/getUsers#Request) endpoint.

parameters.name: sticker_ids
parameters.description: An array of sticker IDs to filter the history results. Only events for these stickers will be included.

parameters.name: user_ids
parameters.description: An array of user IDs to filter by the users who performed the actions.

parameters.name: event_types
parameters.description:
An array of event types to filter the history. Supported values:
- sticker_created: Sticker was created.
- sticker_updated: Sticker was updated.
- sticker_disabled: Sticker was disabled.
- sticker_enabled: Sticker was enabled.
- sticker_deleted: Sticker was deleted.
- sticker_added_to_board: Sticker was added to a board.
- sticker_removed_from_board: Sticker was removed from a board.
- sticker_updated_for_board: Sticker properties were updated for a board.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of sticker history events.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

all_pages.description: The total number of pages in the paginated result set.

current_page.description: The page number of the current set of results.

results_per_page.description: The number of results per page.

data.description: An array of sticker history events, each representing a creation, update, deletion, or board assignment change.

get.summary: Get a list of stickers
get.description: Retrieves a list of stickers, optionally filtered by various criteria.

parameters.name: sticker_ids
parameters.description: An array of sticker IDs to filter the results. Only stickers with these IDs will be returned.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: The full label of the sticker to search for. Returns stickers that match this label exactly.

parameters.name: availability
parameters.description:
An array of availability values to filter stickers. Possible values:
- `0`: On-demand
- `1`: Auto
- `2`: Global

parameters.name: is_enabled
parameters.description: Filter stickers by enabled status. Set to 1 for enabled stickers, 0 for disabled stickers.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
An array of fields to include in the response for each sticker.
Allowed fields: sticker_id, icon_type, icon_id, label, color, availability, is_enabled.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: An array of properties for which you want to get additional details.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of stickers.

schema.description: Sticker data.

data.description: An array of sticker objects matching the filter criteria.

post.description: Create a new sticker.

schema.description: The data required to create a new sticker.

icon_type.description: The type of icon for the sticker. `0` for system icon, `1` for user-defined icon. Defaults to `0`.

icon_id.description: The icon ID for the sticker. If set to `0`, the sticker will not have an icon. Must be a non-negative integer. Defaults to `0`.

label.description: The label or name for the new sticker. Must be between 1 and 250 characters.

color.description: The color of the sticker, represented as a 6-character hexadecimal string (e.g., "eceff1"). Can be empty.

availability.description:
Controls how the sticker is made available on boards:
- `0`: On-demand (must be added to boards manually by workspace managers)
- `1`: Auto (added automatically to all new boards, but can be removed by workspace managers)
- `2`: Global (added automatically to all boards and cannot be removed)
Must be one of: 0, 1, 2. Defaults to `0`.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the sticker is enabled (`1`) or disabled (`0`). Defaults to `1`.

get.summary: Get a list of tags
get.description: Retrieves a list of tags with their relevant details, optionally filtered using various parameters.

parameters.name: tag_ids
parameters.description: Filters results to include only tags with matching IDs.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Filters tags by their exact label (i.e. the name of the tag).

parameters.name: availability
parameters.description:
A list of the availability levels by which to filter the tags.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the tag can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the tag is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the tag is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a tag's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

parameters.name: is_enabled
parameters.description: Filters tags based on whether they are enabled.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: A list of fields to include in the response.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: A list of properties to expand for additional details in the response.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of tags.

post.description: Create a new tag.

schema.description: Tag data.

label.description: The **unique** label (i.e. the name) assigned to the tag.

color.description: The color of the tag in hexadecimal format.

availability.description:
The availability level of the tag.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the tag can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the tag is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the tag is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a tag's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the tag is enabled.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

parameters.name: if_assigned_where_i_am
parameters.description:
Filters users based on whether they are assigned to the same boards as the currently authenticated user.
The user needs to be assigned to **at least one board** to which the currently authenticated user is assigned.

It's possible to get the same user as a result when using this filter with a value of `1` or `0`, if the user is also assigned to other boards to which the currently authenticated user is not assigned.

The currently authenticated user will **always** be returned, regardless of the value of this filter.

parameters.description: A list of properties to expand for additional details in the response.

get.summary: Get all webhooks
get.description:
Get a list of all currently defined webhooks.
You can optionally filter the results by providing one or more board IDs.

You can see a list of all boards using the [/boards](#/operations/getBoards) endpoint.

parameters.name: board_ids
parameters.description: A list of board IDs to filter webhooks by. If not provided, returns webhooks for all boards.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the list of webhooks.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: An array of webhook objects.

post.description:
Creates a new webhook to receive notifications for board events.

You can see a list of all boards using the [/boards](#/operations/getBoards) endpoint.

schema.description: The data to create a webhook.

url.description: The endpoint URL that will receive webhook notifications. Must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS address.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the webhook is enabled (`1`) or disabled (`0`). Disabled webhooks do not send notifications. Defaults to `1`.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board for which the webhook will be triggered. If not set, the webhook will be invoked for events on all boards.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

secret.description: The secret key used to sign webhook payloads. This key is only returned upon creation and should be securely stored by the client.

patch.summary: Update a webhook
patch.description:
Updates the configuration of an existing webhook.
You can change the webhook's URL, enabled status, or the board it is associated with.
If `board_id` is set to `null`, the webhook will receive events from all boards.

Related endpoints:
- To see a list of all webhooks, use the [/webhooks](#/operations/getWebhooks) endpoint.
- To see a list of all boards, use the [/boards](#/operations/getBoards) endpoint.

schema.description: The data to update for the webhook.

url.description: The endpoint URL for webhook notifications. Must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS address.

delete.summary: Delete a webhook
delete.description:
Deletes the specified webhook by its unique identifier.
This operation permanently removes the webhook and stops all future notifications to its URL.

You can see a list of all webhooks using the [/webhooks](#/operations/getWebhooks) endpoint.

get.summary: Get a list of workspaces
get.description:
Retrieves a list of workspaces with their relevant details, optionally filtered using various parameters.
There are two types of workspaces:
  - **Team Workspace** - A collection of Team Kanban Boards where a team collaborates. Typical workspace names include 'Engineering', 'Marketing', 'Operations', 'Design', etc.
  - **Management Workspace** - Allows managers to connect one or more Team Kanban Boards to a single Management Board and manage work across multiple teams.

parameters.name: workspace_ids
parameters.description: Filters results to include only workspaces with matching IDs.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Filters workspaces by their type.

A value of `1` represents a **Team Workspace**, while a value of `2` represents a **Management Workspace**.

parameters.name: is_archived
parameters.description: Filters workspaces based on whether they are archived.

parameters.name: if_workspace_manager
parameters.description: Filters workspaces based on whether the currently authenticated user is a workspace manager.

parameters.name: if_assigned_to_boards
parameters.description: Filters workspaces based on whether the currently authenticated user is assigned to any boards within the workspace.

parameters.name: board_filter_is_archived
parameters.description: Filters boards based on their archived status, when the optional `expand` parameter includes `boards`.

parameters.name: board_filter_if_assigned
parameters.description: Filters boards based on whether the currently authenticated user is assigned to any boards within the workspace, when the optional `expand` parameter includes `boards`.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: A list of fields to include in the response.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: Expand a list of the boards within each workspace. This filter can be used in combination with `board_filter_if_assigned` and `board_filter_is_archived` to filter the boards that are returned.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: A list of workspaces.

workspace_id.description: The unique identifier of the workspace.

name.description: The name of the workspace.

A value of `1` represents a **Team Workspace**, while a value of `2` represents a **Management Workspace**.

get.summary: Get workflow's cycle time columns
get.description:
Get the list of columns in a workflow that are configured to be included in the cycle time calculation for cards.
Cycle time is the total time a card spends in the selected columns, helping you measure process efficiency and identify bottlenecks.
You can configure which columns are counted towards cycle time for each workflow.

Related endpoints:
- List boards: [Get Boards](#operations/getBoards#Request)
- List workflows for a specifed board: [Get Workflows](#operations/getWorkflows#Request)
- Get workflow's effective cycle time columns: [Get Workflow Effective Cycle Time Columns](#operations/getWorkflowEffectiveCycleTimeColumns#Request)

200.description: Successfully retrieved the workflow's cycle time columns.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: An array of columns in the workflow that are included in cycle time calculations.

get.summary: Get workflow's effective cycle time columns
get.description:
Retrieves the list of columns in a workflow that are currently included in the effective cycle time calculation for cards.
Effective cycle time columns reflect the actual columns used for measuring cycle time, based on the current board and workflow configuration.
If no columns are added to the cycle time configuration, all columns are included by default to the effective cycle time.
Use this endpoint to see which columns are actively counted towards cycle time for more accurate analytics.

Related endpoints:
- List boards: [Get Boards](#operations/getBoards#Request)
- List workflows for a specifed board: [Get Workflows](#operations/getWorkflows#Request)
- Get workflow's cycle time columns: [Get Workflow Cycle Time Columns](#operations/getWorkflowCycleTimeColumns#Request)

200.description: Successfully retrieved the workflow's effective cycle time columns.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

data.description: An array of columns in the workflow that are currently included in effective cycle time calculations.

get.summary: Get a list of workflows for the specified board
get.description:
Retrieves a list of workflows for the specified board with their relevant details.
There are three types of workflows:
  - **Cards Workflow** - The Cards Workflow represents the team process. This is the place where all tasks are visualized as Kanban cards. In the Cards workflow, you can create new cards that are either independent or linked to an Initiative.
  - **Initiatives Workflow** - The Initiatives Workflow is the place where you can create Initiatives and track their automated progress. An initiative is a bigger task, epic, or a project, which you have to break down into smaller work items (cards).
  - **Timeline Workflow** - The Timeline Workflow allows you to visualize your Initiatives in a linear calendar view. The Initiative shows duration, progress and project delay.

**All boards** have 1 cards and 1 initiatives workflow **by default**.

You can find a list of all boards by using the [boards endpoint](#operations/getBoards#Request).

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

post.description: Create a new workflow.

position.description: The position of the workflow. A value of `0` indicates the topmost workflow.

is_enabled.description: Determines whether the workflow is enabled.

is_collapsible.description: Indicates if the workflow is collapsible. **All** workflows are collapsible by default.

name.description: The name of the new workflow. If not provided, a default name will be assigned based on the workflow type.

type.description:
The type of the workflow.

A value of `0` represents a **Cards workflow**, a value of `1` represents an **Initiative Workflow** and a value of `2` represents a **Timeline Workflow**.

schema.description: A wrapper for a successful response.

Run advanced card searches backed by OpenSearch. Submit an OpenSearch Query DSL payload in the request body. Use standard OpenSearch parameters like `_source`, `size`, `from`, `sort`, and `query` within the JSON body for full control over search behavior.

For field definitions and end-to-end query examples, see the [OpenSearch Cards Index Documentation ↗](https://baramundi.businessmap.io/openSearchCards).
