type.description:
The type of the workspace.

A value of `1` represents a **Team Workspace**, while a value of `2` represents a **Management Workspace**.

is_archived.description: Determines whether the workspace is archived.

name.description: The name of the workspace.

WorkspaceHistoryEvent.title: Workspace history event
WorkspaceHistoryEvent.description: An event recorded whenever a create, update, or delete action is performed for a workspace. This can include boards, dashboards or workspace managers associated with the workspace.

history_id.description: The unique identifier of a history event for a workspace.

workspace_id.description: The unique identifier of the workspace.

event_type.description: The type of the history event.

user_id.description: The ID of the user who performed the action on the workspace.

details.description:
Contains detailed information about the workspace associated with the event.

Both the `previous_state` and `changes` objects are included for the events of type `updated`, `archived`, `unarchived`, `board_renamed`, `board_archived`, `board_unarchived`. Only the `details` object is included for **all** other event types.

previous_state.description: Contains the state of the workspace before the event, capturing its properties prior to the change.

changes.description: Contains the updated parameters reflecting changes made to the workspace.

time.description: The timestamp indicating when the history event occurred, in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

workspace_id.description: The ID of the workspace to which the board belongs.

is_archived.description: Controls whether the board is archived.

name.description: The name of the board.

description.description: The description of the board.

type.description: The type of the board. A value of `1` indicates a **Kanban board**, while a value of `2` indicates an **AI Canvas**.

revision.description: The revision number of the board. It denotes the number of times edits were made to the board structure. A single revision can include multiple edits, if they were done at the same time.

size_type.description: Controls how the card sizes are entered and displayed. If set to 0, the card size is an input field, which allows any numeric value. If set to 1, the card size is a dropdown list with T-shirt sizes like S, M, L, XL, etc. If set to 2, the card size is a dropdown list with the Fibonacci numbers.

allow_exceeding.description: Controls whether exceeding the WIP limits on the board is allowed. If set to 0, the limits can always be exceeded. If set to 1, the limits can be exceeded only with an explanation. If set to 2, the limits cannot be exceeded.

autoarchive_cards_after.description: Controls number of days before automatically moving all cards from Ready to Archive to Permanent Archive.

limit_type.description: Controls the measurement unit for work limits on the board. When limit type is 0, the board limits are calculated based on cards count. When limit type is 1, the board limits are calculated based on the size of the cards, cards without set size are counted as 1.

allow_repeating_custom_card_ids.description: Controls whether repeating custom card ids are allowed on the board. When the value is 0, the custom ids of the cards have to be unique on the board. When the value is 1 repeating custom card ids are allowed.

is_discard_reason_required.description: Controls whether providing a discard reason is required when discarding a card.

size_formula.description: The formula for calculating card size. It can be set as a **Board level policy**.

deadline_formula.description: The formula for calculating card deadlines. It can be set as a **Board level policy**.

default_sender_user_id.description:
The user ID of the default email sender of the board.

The default sender user ID can be set to the default notifications email adddress `{url}_notifications@businessmap.io` for your account. In this case the user ID will be 10-digits long and won't be visible in the `/users` endpoint

default_receiver_user_id.description:
The user ID of the default email receiver of the board.

The default receiver used ID can be set to the **Reporter** or **Owner** of each card on the board. In this case the user ID will be 10-digits long and won't be visible in the `/users` endpoint.

allowe_generic_blocker.description: Indicates if generic blockers are allowed. If disabled, only specific blockers can be used.

name.description: The name of the setting.

type.description: The data type of the setting's value.

restrictions.description: The restrictions of the setting's value.

min.description: The minimum value of the setting.

max.description: The maximum value of the setting.

multiple_values.description: Indicates whether the setting can have multiple values.

default_value.description: The default value of the setting.

value.description: The current value of the setting.

rule_id.description: The unique identifier of the rule.

name.description: The name of the rule. Multiple rules **can** have the **same** name.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the rule is enabled.

workflow_id.description: The ID of the workflow to which the rule applies.

lane_id.description: The ID of the lane to which the rule applies.

column_id.description: The ID of the column to which the rule applies.

area_id.description: The ID of the merged area to which the rule applies.

conditions.description:
Contains the conditions that the rule applies.

An example of a condition is: **Attachments are present**, which requires all cards to have at least one attachment before moving to the specified workflow, lane, column, or area.

field_type.description: The type of the field added as a condition to the rule. Default fields like **Attachments**, **Author**, etc., are of type `plain`, while custom fields are marked as `custom`.

field_name.description: The name of the field added as a condition to the rule.

operator.description: The operator of the field added as a condition to the rule, e.g. `=` or `!=`.

ignore_if_not_present.description: Determines whether the field added as a condition to the rule should be ignored if not present. This applies **only** for the **Custom ID** field and fields of type `custom`. That way if a card is **missing** a custom ID or a custom field, it can skip the applied condition and still be moved to the specified workflow, lane, column or merged area.

items.description: The values of the field added as a condition to the rule.

nested_conditions.description: Contains nested conditions. Applicable for conditions of type `Parent cards`, `Child cards` and `Relative cards` with the operators `all_match`, `some_match`, `some_do_not_match`, `none_match` and `none_match_or_exist`.

name.description: The name of the rule. Multiple rules **can** have the **same** name.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the rule is enabled.

workflow_id.description: The ID of the workflow to which the rule applies.

lane_id.description: The ID of the lane to which the rule applies.

column_id.description: The ID of the column to which the rule applies. **Cannot** be used in combination with `area_id`.

area_id.description: The ID of the merged area to which the rule applies. **Cannot** be used in combination with `column_id`.

name.description: The name of the rule. Multiple rules **can** have the **same** name.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the rule is enabled.

prompt_id.description: The unique identifier of a BAI prompt.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board where the BAI prompt is used.

type.description: The type of the BAI prompt. **Comments and Emails**, **Subtasks** and **Summaries** prompts correspond respectively to the values `1`, `2` and `3`.

color.description: The color of the BAI prompt in hexadecimal format.

icon_type.description: The type of the icon associated with the BAI prompt. `0` represents a system icon, while `1` represents a user-defined icon.

icon_id.description: The unique identifier for the icon associated with the BAI prompt.

name.description: The **unique** name assigned to the BAI prompt.

context.description: Describes the general situation in which the AI assistant will operate.

topics.description: Contains a list of additional important topics to include in the generated content. This is available **only** for **Comments and Emails** and **Subtasks** BAI prompts.

suggest_missing_topics.description: Indicates whether the AI model can suggesttopics that may be missing from the generated content.

fields.description:
An array of objects that specifies the **card properties** (e.g, Title, Description, Subtasks) to be sent to the AI model for analysis.

The **order** of the objects reflects the **priority**, with the most important properties listed first.

Each object defines a specific field type and name.

field_type.description: Determines the type of field being sent for analysis to the AI model. Default fields like **Title**, **Description**, etc., are of type `plain`, while custom fields are marked as `custom`.

field_name.description: For fields of type `plain`, the name is stated explicitly (e.g., **Title**, **Description**). For fields of type `custom`, the field is represented by the [ID of the custom field](#/operations/getCustomFields#Request).

author_user_id.description: The ID of the user who created the BAI prompt.

type.description: The type of the BAI prompt. **Comments and Emails**, **Subtasks** and **Summaries** prompts correspond respectively to the values `1`, `2` and `3`.

color.description: The color of the BAI prompt in hexadecimal format.

icon_type.description: The type of the icon associated with the BAI prompt. `0` represents a system icon, while `1` represents a user-defined icon.

icon_id.description: The unique identifier for the icon associated with the BAI prompt.

name.description: The **unique** name assigned to the BAI prompt.

context.description: Describes the general situation in which the AI assistant will operate.

topics.description: Contains a list of additional important topics to include in the generated content. This is available **only** for **Comments and Emails** and **Subtasks** BAI prompts.

suggest_missing_topics.description: Indicates whether the AI model can suggesttopics that may be missing from the generated content.

color.description: The color of the BAI prompt in hexadecimal format.

icon_type.description: The type of the icon associated with the BAI prompt. `0` represents a system icon, while `1` represents a user-defined icon.

icon_id.description: The unique identifier for the icon associated with the BAI prompt.

name.description: The **unique** name assigned to the BAI prompt.

context.description: Describes the general situation in which the AI assistant will operate.

topics.description: Contains a list of additional important topics to include in the generated content. This is available **only** for **Comments and Emails** and **Subtasks** BAI prompts.

suggest_missing_topics.description: Indicates whether the AI model can suggesttopics that may be missing from the generated content.

name.description: The name of the rule. Multiple rules **can** have the **same** name.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the rule is enabled.

workflow_id.description: The ID of the workflow to which the rule applies.

lane_id.description: The ID of the lane to which the rule applies.

column_id.description: The ID of the column to which the rule applies.

area_id.description: The ID of the merged area to which the rule applies.

conditions.description:
Contains the conditions that the rule applies.

An example of a condition is: **Attachments are present**, which requires all cards to have at least one attachment before moving to the specified workflow, lane, column, or area.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user to whom the role is assigned.

role_id.description: The unique identifier of the role being assigned to the user.

BoardStructure.title: Board structure
BoardStructure.description: Contains all the information necessary to draw a board: the board details, its settings, workflows, lanes, columns, merged areas and cell limits.

version.description: The version of the board structure. This value is always set to `1.0`.

workspace_id.description: The unique identifier of a workspace. There are two workspace types: **Management** and **Team**.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of a board.

name.description: The name of the board.

description.description: The description of the board.

is_archived.description: Indicates whether the board is archived. Archived boards are no longer active but are retained for reference instead of being permanently deleted.

workflow_order.description: The order of workflows in the board determined by their position in the array.

workflows.description:
A list of workflows defined within a board. Every board in Businessmap includes two default workflows - the **Initiatives Workflow** and the **Cards Workflow**.

The **Cards Workflow** is usually the first workflow in the API (and second workflow in the board UI) and the **Initiatives Workflow** is typically the second workflow in the API (and top workflow in the board UI). Boards can also include a **Timeline Workflow**.

type.description: The type of the workflow. **Cards**, **Initiative** and **Timeline workflows** correspond respectively to the values `0`, `1` and `2`.

position.description: The position of the workflow. The topmost workflow has a position of `0`.

is_enabled.description:
Indicates whether the workflow is currently active.

A value of `1` means the workflow is enabled and visible on the board.

A value of `0` means it is disabled and hidden, though its work items remain accessible via **Advanced Search** and can be re-enabled later.

is_collapsible.description: Indicates if the workflow is collapsible. **All** workflows are collapsible by default.

name.description: The name of the workflow.

top_lanes.description:
An array of lane IDs representing all lanes located at the top of the workflow.

If the workflow has multiple lanes, each lane will be included in this array by its unique identifier.

bottom_lanes.description:
An array of lane IDs that always matches the structure of `top_lanes` of the workflow.

Each ID corresponds to the same lane as in `top_lanes`, ensuring a consistent layout between the top and bottom sections of the workflow.

top_columns.description:
An array of column objects that define the vertical structure of the top section of the workflow.

By default, there should be a single column for each area - **Backlog**, **Requested**, **In Progress**, **Done**, and **Ready to Archive** - but since multiple columns can exist per area, `top_columns` includes only the topmost column for each area in the workflow.

If there are three or more columns for a given area, only the topmost and bottommost columns will be included in the respective `top_columns` and `bottom_columns` arrays.

bottom_columns.description:
An array of column objects that define the vertical structure of the bottom section of the workflow.

By default, there should be a single column for each area - **Backlog**, **Requested**, **In Progress**, **Done**, and **Ready to Archive** - but since multiple columns can exist per area, `bottom_columns` includes only the bottommost column for each area in the workflow.

If there are three or more columns for a given area, only the topmost and bottommost columns will be included in the respective `top_columns` and `bottom_columns` arrays.

section_columns.description:
A mapping of section identifiers to the columns they contain within a workflow.

The five predefined sections are **Backlog**, **Requested**, **In Progress**, **Done**, and **Ready to Archive**. They correspond respectively to the values `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, and `5`.

Each section can contain multiple columns at the same level - such as **Requested 1** and **Requested 2** - or nested structures where child columns are grouped under a parent column.

lanes.description: A list of horizontal lanes defined within a board. By default each workflow has a single lane.

workflow_id.description: The ID of the workflow the lane belongs to.

name.description: The name of the lane.

description.description: The description of the lane.

color.description: The color of the lane in hexadecimal format.

columns.description:
A list of columns defined within a workflow.

They are organized within predefined sections such as **Backlog**, **Requested**, **In Progress**, **Done**, and **Ready to Archive**.

Columns can exist independently at the same level or be structured hierarchically with parent-child relationships.

workflow_id.description: The ID of the workflow the column belongs to.

section.description:
The section of the workflow the column belongs to.

The sections **Backlog**, **Requested**, **In Progress**, **Done**, and **Ready to Archive** correspond respectively to the values `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, and `5`.

parent_column_id.description: The ID of the parent column. It is possible to create hierarchical columns by setting this value to the ID of another column.

name.description: The name of the column.

description.description: The description of the column.

color.description: The color of the column in hexadecimal format.

limit.description: The limit of cards in the column. A value of `0` indicates that there is no limit, allowing an unlimited number of cards in that area.

cards_per_row.description: The number of cards per row in the column.

flow_type.description:
Indicates the flow type of a column, which determines how work is handled in that stage of the process.

A value of `0` represents an **Activity** column, where active work is being performed (e.g., "Tech Design").

A value of `1` represents a **Queue** column, which indicates a waiting stage where no active work occurs (e.g., "Ready for Review").

card_ordering.description:
Cards can be ordered by `block_reason`, `created_at`, `custom_id`, `cycle_time`, `deadline`, `last_modified`, `local_cycle_time`, `owner`, and `priority`.

The direction can either be ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`).

The `field_type` is always set to `plain`.

checklist_items.description:
The IDs of the checklist items in the column.

**Exit criteria** are predefined requirements that must be fulfilled before a card is allowed to move to the next column.

The `column_checklist_items` object contains all the checklist items for the board, keyed by checklist item IDs.

child_columns.description:
Represents the child columns associated with a specific parent column on the board.

This field indicates whether a column has nested sub-columns, allowing for hierarchical organization within the workflow.

column_checklist_items.description:
Checklist items for columns, keyed by checklist item IDs.

**Exit criteria** are predefined requirements that must be fulfilled before a card is allowed to move to the next column (i.e., forward in the workflow).

column_id.description: The ID of the column this checklist item belongs to.

text.description: The name of the exit criterion.

position.description: The position of the checklist item.

size_type.description:
Defines the input format used for the `size` field on cards.

A value of `0` corresponds to **No pattern**, allowing any numeric value.

A value of `1` corresponds to the **Fibonacci sequence**, offering a dropdown of predefined Fibonacci numbers.

A value of `2` corresponds to **T-shirt sizes**, providing labeled options such as S, M, L, XL, etc., internally mapped to numeric values.

allow_exceeding.description:
Determines how exceeding Work-In-Progress (WIP) limits is handled when adding new cards.

A value of `0` permits cards to exceed the WIP limit without restriction.

A value of `1` allows exceeding the limit only if the user provides a reason.

A value of `2` strictly prevents any new cards from being added once the limit is reached.

autoarchive_cards_after.description:
Specifies the number of days a completed card should remain in the **Ready to Archive** column before being automatically archived.

Cards are moved to the **Permanent Archive** automatically after `1`, `7`, `14`, or `30` days, based on the configured value.

limit_type.description:
Specifies how the Work-In-Progress (WIP) limit is calculated for a workflow element, such as a column or lane.

A value of `0` represents **Card Count**, which limits the number of individual cards allowed in a stage regardless of their size.

A value of `1` represents **Card Size**, which restricts the total cumulative size of all cards in a stage based on the size values assigned to them.

allow_repeating_custom_card_ids.description:
Determines whether a Custom card ID can be used only once or multiple times across cards.

The custom card ID settings of the board **Unique value** and **Repeating value** correspond respectively to the values `0` and `1`.

is_discard_reason_required.description: Indicates if a discard reason is required for every card.

size_formula.description: The formula for calculating card size. It can be set as a **Board level policy**.

deadline_formula.description: The formula for calculating card deadlines. It can be set as a **Board level policy**.

default_sender_user_id.description:
The user ID of the default email sender of the board.

The default sender user ID can be set to the default notifications email adddress `{url}_notifications@businessmap.io` for your account. In this case the user ID will be 10-digits long and won't be visible in the `/users` endpoint

default_receiver_user_id.description:
The user ID of the default email receiver of the board.

The default receiver used ID can be set to the **Reporter** or **Owner** of each card on the board. In this case the user ID will be 10-digits long and won't be visible in the `/users` endpoint.

allow_generic_blocker.description: Indicates if generic blockers are allowed. If disabled, only specific blockers can be used.

cell_card_orderings.description:
Displays the cell ordering of columns grouped by lane IDs.

Cards can be ordered by `block_reason`, `created_at`, `custom_id`, `cycle_time`, `deadline`, `last_modified`, `local_cycle_time`, `owner`, and `priority`.

The direction can either be ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`).

The `field_type` is always set to `plain`.

cell_limits.description: Displays the cell limits of columns grouped by lane IDs.

lane_section_limits.description:
Specifies the maximum number of cards allowed in each predefined section of a swimlane, grouped by lane ID.

Each entry maps a swimlane ID to one or more board sections, with the section identifier as the key and the Work-In-Progress (WIP) limit as the value.

The five predefined sections are **Backlog**, **Requested**, **In Progress**, **Done**, and **Ready to Archive**, corresponding respectively to the values `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, and `5`.

merged_areas.description:
Merged areas in the board, keyed by area IDs.

When there is more than one lane or column for an area, cells within it can be merged.

Cells can be merged horizontally when multiple columns are present and vertically when multiple lanes are present.

primary_column_id.description: The primary column ID for the merged area.

limit.description: The limit of cards within the merged area.

card_ordering.description:
Displays the cell ordering of columns grouped by lane IDs.

Cards can be ordered by `block_reason`, `created_at`, `custom_id`, `cycle_time`, `deadline`, `last_modified`, `local_cycle_time`, `owner`, and `priority`.

The direction can either be ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`).

The `field_type` is always set to `plain`.

lane_ids.description: An array of lane IDs included in the merged area.

column_ids.description: An array of column IDs included in the merged area.

revision.description: The revision number denotes the number of times edits were made to the board structure. A single revision can include multiple edits, if they were done at the same time.

version_id.description: The unique identifier of the archived card version.

name.description: The name of the archived card version. Automatically created archived versions will have the name `Autoarchive: YYYY-MM-DD`.

BoardHistoryEvent.title: Board history event
BoardHistoryEvent.description: An event recorded when a board management action is performed for a board.

history_id.description: The unique identifier of a history event for a board.

board_id.description: The unique identifier of a board.

event_type.description: The type of history event.

user_id.description: The ID of the user who performed the action on the board.

details.description:
Contains detailed information about the board associated with the event. The object may greatly vary based on the type of event that occurred.

Updates to the board itself, e.g. changing its description, will result in larger history event containing `previous_state` and `changes`, whereas changes to single parameters, e.g. custom fields, will display details related only to that.

previous state.description: Contains a list of parameteres describing the previous state of the board before the event occurred.

changes.description: Contains a list of parameters describing the changes made to the board during the event.

time.description: The timestamp indicating when the history event occurred, in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

dashboard_page_id.description: The ID of the dashboard page.

name.description: The name of the dashboard page.

DashboardPageCloneData.title: Cloned dashboard page details
DashboardPageCloneData.description: Contains all data returned when a dashboard page is cloned, including its name, unique identifier, and all associated users, teams, workspaces, and widgets.

name.description: The name of the cloned dashboard page.

dashboard_page_id.description: The ID of the cloned dashboard page.

dashboard_page_users.description: A list of users who have access to the cloned dashboard page, including their permissions.

dashboard_page_id.description: The ID of the dashboard page.

user_id.description: The ID of the user with access to the dashboard page.

can_edit.description:
Indicates if the user can manage the dashboard page.
 - `0` means the user can only view the dashboard page.
 - `1` means the user can edit the dashboard page.

items.description: A list of teams with access to the cloned dashboard page, including their permissions.

dashboard_page_id.description: The ID of the dashboard page.

team_id.description: The ID of the team with access to the dashboard page.

can_edit.description:
Indicates if the team can manage the dashboard page.
 - `0` means the team can only view the dashboard page.
 - `1` means the team can edit the dashboard page.

items.description: A list of workspaces linked to the cloned dashboard page, including their positions.

dashboard_page_id.description: The ID of the dashboard page.

workspace_id.description: The ID of the workspace linked to the dashboard page.

position.description: The position of the workspace in the dashboard page.

dashboard_page_widgets.description: A list of widgets included in the cloned dashboard page.

dashboard_page_id.description: The ID of the dashboard page to which the widget belongs.

name.description: The name of the widget.

type.description: The type of the widget.

configuration.description: The configuration of the widget in JSON format.

DashboardPageUser.title: Assign a dashboard member
DashboardPageUser.description: Assign a user to a dashboard page as a **Dashboard member**.

DashboardPageUserAsManager.title: Assign a dashboard manager
DashboardPageUserAsManager.description: Assign a user to a dashboard page as a **Dashboard manager**.

can_edit.description:
Indicates if the user can manage the dashboard page.

A value of `0` represents **Dashboard member**, meaning the user can view the dashboard.

A value of `1` represents **Dashboard manager**, meaning the user can add or remove users, workspaces, and widgets, as well as rename the dashboard.

dashboard_page_id.description: The unique identifier of the dashboard page.

position.description: The position of the dashboard page in the list of dashboard pages. `0` is the topmost page.

type.description:
The type of the workflow.

A value of `0` represents a **Cards workflow**, a value of `1` represents an **Initiative Workflow** and a value of `2` represents a **Timeline Workflow**.

position.description: The position of the workflow. A value of `0` indicates the topmost workflow.

is_enabled.description: Determines whether the workflow is enabled.

is_collapsible.description: Indicates if the workflow is collapsible. **All** workflows are collapsible by default.

name.description: The name of the workflow.

workflow_id.description: The ID of the workflow to which the lane belongs.

parent_lane_id.description: The ID of the parent lane, if the lane is part of a hierarchical structure.

position.description: The position of the lane within the workflow. `0` means the first lane.

name.description: The name of the lane.

description.description: A brief description of the lane's purpose or usage. Defaults to an empty string if not provided.

color.description: The color associated with the lane, represented as a 6-character hexadecimal string or an empty string.

workflow_id.description: The ID of the workflow to which the column belongs.

section.description:
The section of the workflow where the column is located. Valid values are:
 - `1` - Backlog
 - `2` - Requested
 - `3` - Progress
 - `4` - Done
 Adding columns to the **Archive area**, section `5`, is not allowed.

parent_column_id.description: The ID of the parent column, if the column is part of a hierarchical structure.

name.description: The name of the new column.

position.description: The position of the column within the section or its parent.

description.description: A brief description of the column's purpose or usage. Defaults to an empty string if not provided.

color.description: The color associated with the column, represented as a 6-character hexadecimal string or an empty string.

limit.description: The Work-In-Progress (WIP) limit for the column, which restricts the number of cards that can be in the column at any given time. Defaults to `0` if not provided.

cards_per_row.description: The number of cards displayed per row in the column. Must be a positive integer. Defaults to `1` if not provided.

flow_type.description:
The flow type of the column. Valid values are:
 - `1` - Activity
 - `2` - Queue
 Defaults to `1` if not provided.

card_progress.description: Specifies the manually assigned completion percentage for all cards in the column.

card_progress_size_type.description:
The progress roll-up method of the column. This determines how cards in the column contribute to their parents' progress.

A value of `0` represents **Card size with fallback to cumulative size**, meaning the value explicitly set for size will be used. If no size is set, the cumulative size is used instead. If the size is not set and the cumulative size is 0 (for example, the card has no children), the card's size defaults to 1 .

A value of `1` represents **Card size only**, meaning only the value explicitly set for size will be used. If the card's size is not set, it's counted as 1.

A value of `2` represents **Cumulative size with fallback to card size**, meaning the card's cumulative size — the total size of all its child cards - will be used. If the cumulative size is 0, the card's size is used instead. If the size is not set, the card's size is counted as 1.

name.description: The name of the new column.

position.description: The position of the column within the section or its parent.

description.description: A brief description of the column's purpose or usage. Defaults to an empty string if not provided.

color.description: The color associated with the column, represented as a 6-character hexadecimal string or an empty string.

limit.description: The Work-In-Progress (WIP) limit for the column, which restricts the number of cards that can be in the column at any given time. Defaults to `0` if not provided.

cards_per_row.description: The number of cards displayed per row in the column. Must be a positive integer. Defaults to `1` if not provided.

flow_type.description:
The flow type of the column. Valid values are:
 - `1` - Activity
 - `2` - Queue
 Defaults to `1` if not provided.

card_ordering.description: Specifies how cards are ordered within the column. Cards can be ordered by fields such as `block_reason`, `created_at`, `custom_id`, `cycle_time`, `deadline`, `last_modified`, `local_cycle_time`, `owner`, and `priority`. The direction can be either ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`). The `field_type` is always set to `plain`.

card_progress.description: Specifies the manually assigned completion percentage for all cards in the column.

card_progress_size_type.description:
The progress roll-up method of the column. This determines how cards in the column contribute to their parents' progress.

A value of `0` represents **Card size with fallback to cumulative size**, meaning the value explicitly set for size will be used. If no size is set, the cumulative size is used instead. If the size is not set and the cumulative size is 0 (for example, the card has no children), the card's size defaults to 1 .

A value of `1` represents **Card size only**, meaning only the value explicitly set for size will be used. If the card's size is not set, it's counted as 1.

A value of `2` represents **Cumulative size with fallback to card size**, meaning the card's cumulative size — the total size of all its child cards - will be used. If the cumulative size is 0, the card's size is used instead. If the size is not set, the card's size is counted as 1.

workflow_id.description: The ID of the workflow to which the column belongs.

section.description:
The section of the workflow where the column is located. Valid values are `1`, `2`, `3`, `4` or `5`. `Null` means the column is a subcolumn.
 - `1` - Backlog
 - `2` - Requested
 - `3` - Progress
 - `4` - Done
 - `5` - Archive

parent_column_id.description: The ID of the parent column, if the column is part of a hierarchical structure.

position.description: The position of the column within the section.

name.description: The name of the column.

description.description: A brief description of the column's purpose or usage.

color.description: The color associated with the column, represented as a hexadecimal string.

limit.description: The Work-In-Progress (WIP) limit for the column, which restricts the number of cards that can be in the column at any given time.

cards_per_row.description: The number of cards displayed per row in the column.

flow_type.description: The flow type of the column.

card_ordering.description: Specifies how cards are ordered within the column. Cards can be ordered by fields such as `block_reason`, `created_at`, `custom_id`, `cycle_time`, `deadline`, `last_modified`, `local_cycle_time`, `owner`, and `priority`. The direction can be either ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`). The `field_type` is always set to `plain`.

card_progress.description: Specifies the manually assigned completion percentage for all cards in the column.

card_progress_size_type.description:
The progress roll-up method of the column. This determines how cards in the column contribute to their parents' progress.

A value of `0` represents **Card size with fallback to cumulative size**, meaning the value explicitly set for size will be used. If no size is set, the cumulative size is used instead. If the size is not set and the cumulative size is 0 (for example, the card has no children), the card's size defaults to 1 .

A value of `1` represents **Card size only**, meaning only the value explicitly set for size will be used. If the card's size is not set, it's counted as 1.

A value of `2` represents **Cumulative size with fallback to card size**, meaning the card's cumulative size — the total size of all its child cards - will be used. If the cumulative size is 0, the card's size is used instead. If the size is not set, the card's size is counted as 1.

primary_column_id.description: For merged areas spanning multiple columns, this is the ID of the column which will reflect the state of the cards in the merged cells in terms of board positioning.

limit.description: The limit of cards within the merged area, which restricts the number of cards that can be in the merged area at any given time.

card_ordering.description: Specifies how cards are ordered within the merged area. Cards can be ordered by fields such as `block_reason`, `created_at`, `custom_id`, `cycle_time`, `deadline`, `last_modified`, `local_cycle_time`, `owner`, and `priority`. The direction can be either ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`). The `field_type` is always set to `plain`.

lane_ids.description: A list of lane IDs included in the merged area.

column_ids.description: A list of column IDs included in the merged area.

item_id.description: The unique identifier of the checklist item.

text.description: The description of the checklist item.

position.description: The position of the checklist item. `0` is the first position.

allowed_users.description: The list of users allowed to mark the checklist item as completed. If the list is empty, **all** users added to the board are allowed to mark the checklist item as completed.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user allowed to mark the checklist item as completed

board_id.description: The unique identifier of the board.

column_id.description: The unique identifier of the column.

limit.description: The work in progress (WIP) limit of cards in the cell.

section.description: The unique identifier of the section. Since this is an **In Progress** section limit, this is always set to `3`.

cycle_time_column_ids.description: Contains a list of the IDs of the workflow's cycle time columns.

initiative_workflow_settings.description: A list of **Initiative Workflow** settings for the copied workflow. These do **not** apply for **Cards** or **Timeline** workflows.

workflow_id.description: The unique identifier of the **Initiative Workflow**.

built_in_rules_can_start_initiatives.description: Determines whether initiatives are started (moved to the **In Progress** column) when their first child is started.

built_in_rules_can_finish_initiatives.description: Determines whether initiatives are finished (moved to the **Done** column) when all their children are finished.

built_in_rules_can_move_to_requested.description: Determines whether initiatives are moved to the **Requested** column when all their children are in the **Requested** column.

built_in_rules_can_move_back_from_done.description: Determines whether initiatives are outside the **Done** column if one their children is moved outside the **Done** column.

built_in_rules_can_move_from_backlog_to_.description: Determines whether initiatives are moved from the **Backlog** column to the **Requested** column when their first child is moved to the **Requested** column.

built_in_rules_can_move_from_done_to_arc.description: Determines whether initiatives can be moved from the **Done** column to the **Ready to Archive** column.

workflow_template_id.description: The ID of the workflow template.

lane_templates.description: A map where keys are lane IDs and values are template IDs.

items.description:
If an integer is provided, the date will be calculated as `X` days in the future (for positive values) or `X` days in the past (for negative values).

For custom timeframes, you can specify the duration using the following formats as a string: `"X days"`, `"X hours"`, `"X minutes"`, or `"X seconds"`, where `X` can be an integer or decimal and can be positive or negative.

Additionally, if a string is used, any of the following date formats will be accepted: 
- `YYYY-MM-DD` 
- `YYYY-MM-DD HH-MM-SS`
  
You can also use natural language terms like `today`, `tomorrow`, `yesterday`, `next week`, `last week`, `this week`, `last month`, `this month`, and `next month`.

values.description:
The values used in the condition must follow specific patterns.

- Use `"Av:{integer}"` for allowed values of custom fields, where `{integer}` is the ID of the value.
- Use `"Ov:{string}"` for other values of custom fields, where `{string}` can be any value.

The `{integer}` must be a valid ID of an allowed value for a custom field (you can check the allowed values of a custom field by calling the [/customFields/{field_id}/allowedValues](#/operations/getCustomFieldAllowedValues#Request) endpoint), while `{string}` can represent any string.

time.description: The timestamp indicating when the history event occurred, in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

name.description: The **unique** name of the role.

reason_id.description: The unique identifier of the block reason.

icon_type.description: The type of the icon associated with the block reason. `0` represents a system icon, while `1` represents a user-defined icon.

icon_id.description: The unique identifier for the icon associated with the block reason.

label.description: The **unique** label (i.e. the name) assigned to the block reason.

color.description: The color of the block reason in hexadecimal format.

availability.description:
The availability level of the block reason.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the block reason can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the block reason is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the block reason is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a block reason's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the block reason is enabled.

RoleHistoryEvent.title: Role history event
RoleHistoryEvent.description: An event recorded whenever a create, update, or delete action is performed for a role.

history_id.description: The unique identifier of a history event for a role.

role_id.description: The unique identifier of a role.

event_type.description: The type of the history event.

user_id.description: The ID of the user who performed the action on the role.

details.description:
Contains detailed information about the role associated with the event.

The `previous_state` and `changes` objects are included for the events of type `updated`. Only the `details` object is included for **all** other event types.

For the `permissions_changed` event the `details` object can contain the `added` and `removed` arrays which describe the changes to the permissions of the role.

previous_state.description: Contains the state of the role before the event, capturing its properties prior to the change.

changes.description: Contains the updated parameters reflecting changes made to the role.

added.description: An array of permissions that were added to the role.

removed.description: An array of permissions that were removed from the role.

time.description: The timestamp indicating when the history event occurred, in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

reason_id.description: The unique identifier of the discard reason.

label.description: The **unique** label (i.e. the name) assigned to the discard reason.

availability.description:
The availability level of the discard reason.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the discard reason can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the discard reason is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the discard reason is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a discard reason's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the discard reason is enabled.

icon_type.description: The type of icon associated with the sticker. `0` for system icon, `1` for user-defined icon.

icon_id.description: The ID of the icon used for the sticker.

label.description: The name or label of the sticker.

color.description: The color of the sticker, represented as a hexadecimal string.

availability.description:
The availability level of the sticker:
- `0`: On-demand (manually added/removed from boards)
- `1`: Auto (automatically added to new boards)
- `2`: Global (added to all boards across all workspaces)

is_enabled.description: Indicates whether the sticker is enabled (`1`) or disabled (`0`).

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the tag is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a tag's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

OutcomeId.description: Outcome data. When returned by GET by id, outcome_id is omitted (the id is in the path).

starting_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the initial value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the starting value, the value will be set to `null`.

target_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the target value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the target value, the value will be set to `null`.

current_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the current value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the current value, the value will be set to `null`.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the milestone is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a milestone's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

planned_start_date_sync_type.description:
Specifies how the planned start date of the new card is determined. This option is available only for portfolio initiatives and cannot be used together with the `planned_start_date` parameter.
- `1`: The planned start date will be inherited as the earliest start date from all child initiatives in the hierarchy (excluding cards).
- `2`: The planned start date will be inherited from a child initiative or card.
- `3`: The planned start date will be inherited from a child initiative or card, with adjustments for working days.
Use this parameter to automatically sync the planned start date based on the dates of child items.

planned_start_date_sync_monday_is_workin.description: Indicates whether Monday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_tuesday_is_worki.description: Indicates whether Tuesday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_wednesday_is_wor.description: Indicates whether Wednesday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_thursday_is_work.description: Indicates whether Thursday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_friday_is_workin.description: Indicates whether Friday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_saturday_is_work.description: Indicates whether Saturday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_sunday_is_workin.description: Indicates whether Sunday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date.description: The planned start date of the initiative. Cannot be used together with `planned_start_date_sync_type`.

planned_end_date_sync_type.description:
Specifies how the planned end date of the new card is determined. This option is available only for portfolio initiatives and cannot be used together with the `planned_end_date` parameter.
- `1`: The planned end date will be inherited as the latest end date from all child initiatives in the hierarchy (excluding cards).
- `2`: The planned end date will be inherited from a child initiative or card.
Use this parameter to automatically sync the planned end date based on the dates of child items.

planned_end_date.description: The planned end date of the initiative. Cannot be used together with `planned_end_date_sync_type`.

actual_start_time.description: The actual start time of the initiative, in `ISO 8601` format.

actual_end_time.description: The actual end time of the initiative, in `ISO 8601` format.

CardCustomFieldAddOrUpdateRequest.description: Cards custom field data required to add or update a custom field on a card.

value.description:
The value of custom field.
Applicable to `single-line text`, `multi-line text`, `number`, `date`, and `link` custom fields.

vote.description: Your vote for the custom field. `0` represents a negative vote, and `1` represents a positive vote. Applicable to `vote` custom fields.

comment.description: A comment associated with your vote for the custom field. Applicable to `vote` custom fields.

selected_cards_to_add_or_update.description: A list of selected card IDs to add or update in the custom field. Applicable to `card picker` custom fields.

selected_card_ids_to_remove.description: A list of selected card IDs to remove from the custom field. Applicable to `card picker` custom fields.

starting_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the initial value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the starting value, the value will be set to `null`.

The formula type for calculating the target value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the target value, the value will be set to `null`.

current_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the current value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the current value, the value will be set to `null`.

planned_start_date_sync_type.description:
Specifies how the planned start date of the card is determined. This option is available only for portfolio initiatives and cannot be used together with the `planned_start_date` parameter.
- `1`: The planned start date will be inherited as the earliest start date from all child initiatives in the hierarchy (excluding cards).
- `2`: The planned start date will be inherited from a child initiative or card.
- `3`: The planned start date will be inherited from a child initiative or card, with adjustments for working days.
Use this parameter to automatically sync the planned start date based on the dates of child items.

planned_start_date_sync_monday_is_workin.description: Indicates whether Monday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_tuesday_is_worki.description: Indicates whether Tuesday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_wednesday_is_wor.description: Indicates whether Wednesday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_thursday_is_work.description: Indicates whether Thursday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_friday_is_workin.description: Indicates whether Friday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_saturday_is_work.description: Indicates whether Saturday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date_sync_sunday_is_workin.description: Indicates whether Sunday is considered a working day when syncing the planned start date.

planned_start_date.description: The planned start date of the updated card, represented in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

planned_end_date_sync_type.description:
Specifies how the planned end date of the card is determined. This option is available only for portfolio initiatives and cannot be used together with the `planned_end_date` parameter.
- `1`: The planned end date will be inherited as the latest end date from all child initiatives in the hierarchy (excluding cards).
- `2`: The planned end date will be inherited from a child initiative or card.
Use this parameter to automatically sync the planned end date based on the dates of child items.

planned_end_date.description: The planned end date of the converted card, represented in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

A value of `1` corresponds to `Critical` priority.

A value of `2` corresponds to `High` priority.

A value of `3` corresponds to `Average` priority.

A value of `4` corresponds to `Low` priority.

A `null` value will be considered as `Average` priority and any value above `4` will be considered as `Low` priority.

availability.description:
The availability level of the card type.

A value of `0` represents **On-demand**, meaning the card type can be manually added to or removed from boards.

A value of `1` represents **Auto**, meaning the card type is automatically added to all newly created boards.

A value of `2` represents **Global**, meaning the card type is automatically added to all boards across all workspaces. Once a card type's availability is set to **Global**, only an Owner can change its availability again.

custom_id.description: The custom ID of the card.

owner_user_id.description: The ID of the user who is assigned to the card.

type_id.description: The ID of the type assigned of the card.

size.description: The size of the card. A size of `null` will be counted as `1`.

priority.description:
Determines the card's priority.

A value of `1` corresponds to `Critical` priority.

A value of `2` corresponds to `High` priority.

A value of `3` corresponds to `Average` priority.

A value of `4` corresponds to `Low` priority.

A `null` value will be considered as `Average` priority and any value above `4` will be considered as `Low` priority.

color.description: The color of the card in hexadecimal format.

deadline_monday_is_working_day.description: Indicates whether Monday is considered a working day when calculating the deadline.

deadline_tuesday_is_working_day.description: Indicates whether Tuesday is considered a working day when calculating the deadline.

deadline_wednesday_is_working_day.description: Indicates whether Wednesday is considered a working day when calculating the deadline.

deadline_saturday_is_working_day.description: Indicates whether Saturday is considered a working day when calculating the deadline.

deadline_sunday_is_working_day.description: Indicates whether Sunday is considered a working day when calculating the deadline.

watchers_are_visible.description: Controls whether the watchers field is displayed in the card template, even if no users are assigned as watchers.

card_custom_id_is_visible.description: Controls whether the custom id field is displayed in the card template, even if no value is assigned.

milestones_are_visible.description: Controls whether the milestones field is displayed in the card template, even if no milestones are assigned.

stickers_to_add.description: A list of stickers which will be added to the card.

sticker_ids_to_remove.description: A list of sticker IDs which will be removed from the card. These are the values returned by GET|POST /cardTemplates/{template_id}/cards/{card_id}/stickers in the 'id' property.

watcher_ids_to_remove.description: A list of watcher IDs which will be removed from the card.

custom_fields_to_add_or_update.description: A list of custom fields which will be added to or updated on the card.

CreateManualOutcomeOwnValueRequest.title: Create Manual Outcome With Own Value
CreateManualOutcomeOwnValueRequest.description:
A list of outcomes to add. If neither `field_id` nor `rule_id` is provided, the outcome will be created as type **Own value**.

If you want to create an outcome of type **Automatic** using a formula, you can provide all three parameters - `starting_value_formula`, `current_value_formula`, and `target_value_formula`.

Alternatively, if you do not need a custom formula, you can use predefined formulas (such as **SUM** or **AVERAGE**) that automatically calculate values based on referenced outcomes. To use a predefined formula, set the appropriate parameter ( `starting_value_predefined_formula_type`, `current_value_predefined_formula_type`, or `target_value_predefined_formula_type`) to the desired formula type.

name.description: The name of the outcome to be added.

starting_value.description: The initial value assigned to the outcome.

current_value.description: The current value assigned to the outcome.

target_value.description: The target value assigned to the outcome.

operator.description: The operator of the outcome. Choose the operator based on whether you aim to **increase** or **decrease** the outcome.

weight.description: Used to calculate overall progress for initiatives with multiple outcomes. This positive numeric value (not a percentage) represents the relative importance of each outcome.

comment.description: A summary of the outcome, explaining why it's important.

checkpoints_to_add.description: A list of checkpoints which will be added to the outcome.

CreateManualOutcomeWithCustomFieldReques.title: Create Manual Outcome With Custom Field
CreateManualOutcomeWithCustomFieldReques.description:
A list of outcomes to add. If neither `field_id` nor `rule_id` is provided, the outcome will be created as type **Own value**.

If you want to create an outcome of type **Automatic** using a formula, you can provide all three parameters - `starting_value_formula`, `current_value_formula`, and `target_value_formula`.

Alternatively, if you do not need a custom formula, you can use predefined formulas (such as **SUM** or **AVERAGE**) that automatically calculate values based on referenced outcomes. To use a predefined formula, set the appropriate parameter ( `starting_value_predefined_formula_type`, `current_value_predefined_formula_type`, or `target_value_predefined_formula_type`) to the desired formula type.

name.description: The name of the outcome to be added. You can enter a name for outcomes that use a custom field, but the UI will **always** display the name of the associated **custom field** instead.

field_id.description: The ID of the custom field which the outcome will use for calculation.

starting_value.description: The initial value assigned to the outcome.

current_value.description: The current value assigned to the outcome.

target_value.description: The target value assigned to the outcome.

operator.description: The operator of the outcome. Choose the operator based on whether you aim to **increase** or **decrease** the outcome.

weight.description: Used to calculate overall progress for initiatives with multiple outcomes. This positive numeric value (not a percentage) represents the relative importance of each outcome.

comment.description: A summary of the outcome, explaining why it's important.

checkpoints_to_add.description: A list of checkpoints which will be added to the outcome.

CreateAutomaticOutcomeWithRuleRequest.title: Create Automatic Outcome With Outcome Rule
CreateAutomaticOutcomeWithRuleRequest.description:
A list of outcomes to add. If neither `field_id` nor `rule_id` is provided, the outcome will be created as type **Own value**.

If you want to create an outcome of type **Automatic** using a formula, you can provide all three parameters - `starting_value_formula`, `current_value_formula`, and `target_value_formula`.

Alternatively, if you do not need a custom formula, you can use predefined formulas (such as **SUM** or **AVERAGE**) that automatically calculate values based on referenced outcomes. To use a predefined formula, set the appropriate parameter ( `starting_value_predefined_formula_type`, `current_value_predefined_formula_type`, or `target_value_predefined_formula_type`) to the desired formula type.

name.description: The name of the outcome to be added. You can enter a name for outcomes that use an outcome rule, but the UI will **always** display the name of the associated **outcome rule** instead.

rule_id.description: The ID of the outcome rule which the outcome will use for calculation. This should be used **only** if you want to create an outcome of type **Automatic** with an **Outcome rule**.

starting_value.description: The initial value assigned to the outcome.

target_value.description: The target value assigned to the outcome.

operator.description: The operator of the outcome. Choose the operator based on whether you aim to **increase** or **decrease** the outcome.

weight.description: Used to calculate overall progress for initiatives with multiple outcomes. This positive numeric value (not a percentage) represents the relative importance of each outcome.

comment.description: A summary of the outcome, explaining why it's important.

checkpoints_to_add.description: A list of checkpoints which will be added to the outcome.

CreateAutomaticOutcomeWithFormulaRequest.title: Create Automatic Outcome With Formula
CreateAutomaticOutcomeWithFormulaRequest.description:
A list of outcomes to add. If neither `field_id` nor `rule_id` is provided, the outcome will be created as type **Own value**.

If you want to create an outcome of type **Automatic** using a formula, you can provide all three parameters - `starting_value_formula`, `current_value_formula`, and `target_value_formula`.

Alternatively, if you do not need a custom formula, you can use predefined formulas (such as **SUM** or **AVERAGE**) that automatically calculate values based on referenced outcomes. To use a predefined formula, set the appropriate parameter ( `starting_value_predefined_formula_type`, `current_value_predefined_formula_type`, or `target_value_predefined_formula_type`) to the desired formula type.

name.description: The name of the outcome to be added.

starting_value_formula.description: The formula for calculating the initial value of the outcome.

target_value_formula.description: The formula for calculating the target value of the outcome.

current_value_formula.description: The formula for calculating the current value of the outcome.

starting_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the initial value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the starting value, the value will be set to `null`.

target_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the target value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the target value, the value will be set to `null`.

current_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the current value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the current value, the value will be set to `null`.

operator.description: The operator of the outcome. Choose the operator based on whether you aim to **increase** or **decrease** the outcome.

weight.description: Used to calculate overall progress for initiatives with multiple outcomes. This positive numeric value (not a percentage) represents the relative importance of each outcome.

comment.description: A summary of the outcome, explaining why it's important.

checkpoints_to_add.description: A list of checkpoints which will be added to the outcome.

OutcomeCreateRequestBase.description: Common fields for outcome create. Source is one of field_id or rule_id (mutually exclusive). For starting/target/current value use exactly one of numeric value, formula string, or predefined formula type per slot.

card_id.description: Card to attach the outcome to.

name.description: Name of the outcome. Required when not using field_id or rule_id.

operator.description: Operator for the outcome.

target_time.description: Target time (required); must be after starting_time.

starting_value.description: Starting value. Mutually exclusive with starting_value_formula and starting_value_predefined_formula_type.

target_value.description: Target value. Mutually exclusive with target_value_formula and target_value_predefined_formula_type.

current_value.description: Current value. Mutually exclusive with current_value_formula and current_value_predefined_formula_type; not used with field_id or rule_id.

starting_value_formula.description: Custom formula for starting value. Mutually exclusive with starting_value and starting_value_predefined_formula_type; not used with field_id or rule_id.

target_value_formula.description: Custom formula for target value. Mutually exclusive with target_value and target_value_predefined_formula_type; not used with field_id or rule_id.

current_value_formula.description: Custom formula for current value. Mutually exclusive with current_value and current_value_predefined_formula_type; not used with field_id or rule_id.

starting_value_predefined_formula_type.description: Predefined formula for starting value (1=Min, 2=Max, 3=Sum, 4=Average). Mutually exclusive with starting_value and starting_value_formula; not used with field_id or rule_id.

target_value_predefined_formula_type.description: Predefined formula for target value (1=Min, 2=Max, 3=Sum, 4=Average). Mutually exclusive with target_value and target_value_formula; not used with field_id or rule_id.

current_value_predefined_formula_type.description: Predefined formula for current value (1=Min, 2=Max, 3=Sum, 4=Average). Mutually exclusive with current_value and current_value_formula; not used with field_id or rule_id.

reference.description: Reference for linking (e.g. when adding to a card). Required when creating linked outcomes.

OutcomeUpdateRequestBase.description:
Common optional fields for outcome update (PATCH /outcomes/{id}).
When changing outcome type, send at most one of field_id or rule_id (see oneOf variants).
For starting value use at most one of starting_value, starting_value_formula, or starting_value_predefined_formula_type.
For target value use at most one of target_value, target_value_formula, or target_value_predefined_formula_type.
For current value use at most one of current_value, current_value_formula, or current_value_predefined_formula_type; do not set current_value when using field_id or rule_id.

CardTemplateCustomFieldFile.description: Represents the data for a file associated with a custom field in a card within a card template.

CardBlockReason.title: Card block reason
CardBlockReason.description: Represents the block reason data for a card. This can include either a generic block reason or a user-defined block reason.

comment.description: An optional comment explaining the block reason.

reason_id.description: The ID of the block reason, if applicable.

CardGenericBlockReason.title: Set a Generic Block Reason
CardGenericBlockReason.description: Represents the data required to set a generic block reason for a card. Generic block reasons are predefined and do not require a specific reason ID.

comment.description: A required comment explaining the reason for blocking the card. This provides additional context for the block.

reason_id.description: The ID of the block reason. This field is always `null` for generic blockers, as they do not require a specific reason ID.

CardUserDefinedBlockReason.title: Set a User-Defined Block Reason
CardUserDefinedBlockReason.description: Represents the data required to set a user-defined block reason for a card. User-defined block reasons allow users to specify custom reasons for blocking a card.

comment.description: An optional comment explaining the reason for blocking the card. This provides additional context for the block and is useful for tracking purposes.

reason_id.description: The unique ID of the block reason. This field is required when setting a user-defined block reason to identify the specific reason for the block.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the comment.

type.description:
The type of the comment, indicating how it was created or received:
- `plain`: A standard comment added directly to the card.
- `sent as email`: A comment created when an email was sent from the card.
- `received as email`: A comment created when an email was received on the card.

text.description: The content of the comment, which may include plain text or email content.

attachments.description: A list of attachments associated with the comment.

created_at.description: The date and time when the comment was created, in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

last_modified.description: The date and time when the comment was last modified, in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

CardCommentAuthor.title: Card comment author
CardCommentAuthor.description: Represents the data for comment created internally within the system.

type.description: The type of the author.

value.description: The unique identifier of the author.

author.description: The author of the comment, including their type and their unique identifier. For internal comments, the type is always `internal`, and the value is the user ID of the author.

CardCommentSenderAndRecipients.title: Card comment sender and recipients
CardCommentSenderAndRecipients.description: Represents the data for comments sent or received as emails.

CardCommentSender.title: Card comment sender
CardCommentSender.description:
Represents the data for the sender of a card comment.
This is used for comments sent as emails.

sender.description:
The sender of the comment, including their type (internal or external)
and their unique identifier.

type.description:
The type of the sender:
- `internal`: The sender is an internal user.
- `external`: The sender is an external user.

value.description: The unique identifier of the sender, which can be an **integer** ID for internal users or a **string** email address for external users.

CardCommentRecipients.title: Card comment recipients
CardCommentRecipients.description:
Represents the data for the recipients of a card comment.
This includes the recipients in the `to`, `cc`, and `bcc` fields.

recipients.description:
The recipients of the comment, categorized into `to`, `cc`, and `bcc` fields.
Each recipient includes their type (internal or external) and their unique identifier.

to.description: A list of primary recipients of the comment.

type.description:
The type of the recipient:
- `internal`: The recipient is an internal user.
- `external`: The recipient is an external user.

value.description: The unique identifier of the sender, which can be an **integer** ID for internal users or a **string** email address for external users.

cc.description: A list of recipients copied on the comment.

type.description:
The type of the recipient:
- `internal`: The recipient is an internal user.
- `external`: The recipient is an external user.

value.description: The unique identifier of the sender, which can be an **integer** ID for internal users or a **string** email address for external users.

bcc.description: A list of recipients blind copied on the comment.

type.description:
The type of the recipient:
- `internal`: The recipient is an internal user.
- `external`: The recipient is an external user.

value.description: The unique identifier of the sender, which can be an **integer** ID for internal users or a **string** email address for external users.

type.description: The type of the comment. Only `plain` type is supported for creating comments through the api.

attachments.description: A list of attachments associated with the comment.

created_at.description: The date and time when the comment was created, in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

last_modified.description: The date and time when the comment was last modified, in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the comment.

CardOutcomeListItem.title: Card outcome list item
CardOutcomeListItem.description: Outcome linked to a card (outcome_id and position) as returned by GET /cards/{card_id}/cardOutcomes.

outcome_id.description: The unique identifier of the outcome.

position.description: The position of the outcome in the card's outcome list. `0` is the first position.

CardOutcomePositionResponse.title: Card outcome position
CardOutcomePositionResponse.description: Position of an outcome linked to a card, as returned by GET/PUT /cards/{card_id}/cardOutcomes/{outcome_id}.

position.description: The position of the outcome in the card's outcome list. `0` is the first position.

CardOutcomePositionSetRequest.title: Card outcome position set request
CardOutcomePositionSetRequest.description: Request body for updating the position of an outcome linked to a card.

position.description: The new position (non-negative integer). `0` is the first position.

outcome_id.description: The unique identifier of the outcome.

field_id.description: The ID of the custom field which the outcome will use for calculation. This parameter remains `null` unless there's an outcome of type **Manual** with a **Custom field**.

rule_id.description: The ID of the outcome rule which the outcome will use for calculation. This parameter remains `null` unless there's an outcome of type **Automatic** with an **Outcome Rule**.

name.description:
The name of the outcome.

This field will be set to `null` if the outcome is of type **Automatic** and uses an **Outcome Rule**, or if it is of type **Manual** and uses a **Custom Field**. In those cases, the name will be taken from the Outcome Rule or Custom Field.

operator.description: The operator of the outcome. The operator is based on whether you aim to **increase** or **decrease** the outcome.

starting_value.description: The initial value assigned to the outcome. If you are using an outcome of type **Automatic** with a **Formula** the starting value will be calculated automatically using the `starting_value_formula`.

target_value.description: The target value assigned to the outcome. If you are using an outcome of type **Automatic** with a **Formula** the target value will be calculated automatically using the `target_value_formula`.

current_value.description: The current value assigned to the outcome. If you are using an outcome of type **Automatic** with an **Outcome Rule** the current value will be calculated automatically. If you are using an outcome of type **Automatic** with a **Formula** the current value will be calculated automatically using the `current_value_formula`.

comment.description: A summary of the outcome, explaining why it's important.

starting_value_formula.description: The formula for calculating the initial value of the outcome. This is used **only** for outcomes of type **Automatic** with a **Formula**, otherwise it remains `null`.

target_value_formula.description: The formula for calculating the target value of the outcome. This is used **only** for outcomes of type **Automatic** with a **Formula**, otherwise it remains `null`.

current_value_formula.description: The formula for calculating the current value of the outcome.  This is used **only** for outcomes of type **Automatic** with a **Formula**, otherwise it remains `null`.

starting_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the initial value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the starting value, the value will be set to `null`.

This is used **only** for outcomes of type **Automatic** with a **Formula**, otherwise it remains `null`.

target_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the target value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the target value, the value will be set to `null`.

This is used **only** for outcomes of type **Automatic** with a **Formula**, otherwise it remains `null`.

current_value_predefined_formula_type.description:
The formula type for calculating the current value of the outcome.
A value of `1` represents **Min**, `2` represents **Max**, `3` represents **Sum**, and `4` represents **Average**. If a **Custom formula** is used for calculating the current value, the value will be set to `null`.

This is used **only** for outcomes of type **Automatic** with a **Formula**, otherwise it remains `null`.

weight.description: Used to calculate overall progress for initiatives with multiple outcomes. This positive numeric value (not a percentage) represents the relative importance of each outcome.

created_at.description: The date and time when the outcome was created in `ISO 8601` format with timezone offset.

checkpoints.description: Contains a list of the checkpoints set as intermediate targets for the outcome.

links_to_cards.description: Contains a list of the links to cards associated with the outcome. Each reference to another outcome's value is added as a **separate** link.

links_to_outcomes.description:
Contains a list of the links to outcomes associated with the outcome. It allows the values of other outcomes to be used in the formula. Usually the outcomes of **parent** cards have links to their children's outcomes.

Each reference to another outcome's value is added as a **separate** link. This is used **only** for outcomes of type **Automatic** with a **Formula**.

links_from_outcomes.description:
Contains a list of the links from outcomes associated with the outcome. It allows the values of this outcome to be used in the formulas of other outcomes. Usually the outcomes of **child** cards have links from their parents' outcomes.

Each reference to another outcome's value is added as a **separate** link. This is used **only** for outcomes of type **Automatic** with a **Formula**.
